     114                 :            : 
     115                 :            : /*
     116                 :            :  * XLOGfileslop is the maximum number of preallocated future XLOG segments.
     117                 :            :  * When we are done with an old XLOG segment file, we will recycle it as a
     118                 :            :  * future XLOG segment as long as there aren't already XLOGfileslop future
     119                 :            :  * segments; else we'll delete it.  This could be made a separate GUC
     120                 :            :  * variable, but at present I think it's sufficient to hardwire it as
     121                 :            :  * 2*CheckPointSegments+1.      Under normal conditions, a checkpoint will free
     122                 :            :  * no more than 2*CheckPointSegments log segments, and we want to recycle all
     123                 :            :  * of them; the +1 allows boundary cases to happen without wasting a
     124                 :            :  * delete/create-segment cycle.
     125                 :            :  */
     126                 :            : #define XLOGfileslop    (2*CheckPointSegments + 1)
     127                 :            : 
     128                 :            : 
     129                 :            : /* these are derived from XLOG_sync_method by assign_xlog_sync_method */
     130                 :            : int                     sync_method = DEFAULT_SYNC_METHOD;
     131                 :            : static int      open_sync_bit = DEFAULT_SYNC_FLAGBIT;
     132                 :            : 
     133                 :            : /*
     134                 :            :  * walreceiver process receives xlog data from walsender process.
     135                 :            :  * It needs to write the xlog data as soon as it receives and the amount it receives.
     136                 :            :  * As the amount of data received by it to write cannot be guaranteed to be
     137                 :            :  * OS/FS block size aligned, should never use O_DIRECT for the same.
     138                 :            :  * Also, as code is not expecting O_DIRECT to be used for xlog writes on walreceiver,
     139                 :            :  * the buffer pointer to perform xlog writes is not made usre to be OS/FS blocks size aligned.
     140                 :            :  */
     141                 :            : #define XLOG_SYNC_BIT  (enableFsync && (MyAuxProcType != WalReceiverProcess) ? \
     142                 :            :                                                 open_sync_bit : 0)
     143                 :            : 
     144                 :            : bool am_startup = false;
     145                 :            : 
     146                 :            : /*
     147                 :            :  * Statistics for current checkpoint are collected in this global struct.
     148                 :            :  * Because only the background writer or a stand-alone backend can perform
     149                 :            :  * checkpoints, this will be unused in normal backends.
     150                 :            :  */
     151                 :            : CheckpointStatsData CheckpointStats;

     153                 :            : /*
     154                 :            :  * ThisTimeLineID will be same in all backends --- it identifies current
     155                 :            :  * WAL timeline for the database system.
     156                 :            :  */
     157                 :            : TimeLineID      ThisTimeLineID = 0;

     159                 :            : /* Are we doing recovery from XLOG? */
     160                 :            : bool            InRecovery = false;

     162                 :            : /*
     163                 :            :  * Local copy of SharedRecoveryInProgress variable. True actually means "not
     164                 :            :  * known, need to check the shared state".
     165                 :            :  */
     166                 :            : static bool LocalRecoveryInProgress = true;
     167                 :            :

     169                 :            : static bool restoredFromArchive = false;
     170                 :            : 
     171                 :            : /* options taken from recovery.conf */
     172                 :            : static bool recoveryTarget = false;
     173                 :            : static bool recoveryTargetExact = false;
     174                 :            : static bool recoveryTargetInclusive = true;
     175                 :            : static bool recoveryLogRestartpoints = false;
     176                 :            : static TransactionId recoveryTargetXid;
     177                 :            : static TimestampTz recoveryTargetTime;
     178                 :            : static TimestampTz recoveryLastXTime = 0;
     179                 :            : 
     180                 :            : /* options taken from recovery.conf for XLOG streaming */
     181                 :            : static bool StandbyModeRequested = false;
     182                 :            : static char *PrimaryConnInfo = NULL;
     183                 :            : 
     184                 :            : /* are we currently in standby mode? */
     185                 :            : bool StandbyMode = false;
     186                 :            : 
     187                 :            : /* if recoveryStopsHere returns true, it saves actual stop xid/time here */
     188                 :            : static TransactionId recoveryStopXid;
     189                 :            : static TimestampTz recoveryStopTime;
     190                 :            : static bool recoveryStopAfter;
     191                 :            : 
     192                 :            : /*
     193                 :            :  * During normal operation, the only timeline we care about is ThisTimeLineID.
     194                 :            :  * During recovery, however, things are more complicated.  To simplify life
     195                 :            :  * for rmgr code, we keep ThisTimeLineID set to the "current" timeline as we
     196                 :            :  * scan through the WAL history (that is, it is the line that was active when
     197                 :            :  * the currently-scanned WAL record was generated).  We also need these
     198                 :            :  * timeline values:
     199                 :            :  *
     200                 :            :  * recoveryTargetTLI: the desired timeline that we want to end in.
     201                 :            :  *
     202                 :            :  * expectedTLIs: an integer list of recoveryTargetTLI and the TLIs of
     203                 :            :  * its known parents, newest first (so recoveryTargetTLI is always the
     204                 :            :  * first list member).  Only these TLIs are expected to be seen in the WAL
     205                 :            :  * segments we read, and indeed only these TLIs will be considered as
     206                 :            :  * candidate WAL files to open at all.
     207                 :            :  *
     208                 :            :  * curFileTLI: the TLI appearing in the name of the current input WAL file.
     209                 :            :  * (This is not necessarily the same as ThisTimeLineID, because we could
     210                 :            :  * be scanning data that was copied from an ancestor timeline when the current
     211                 :            :  * file was created.)  During a sequential scan we do not allow this value
     212                 :            :  * to decrease.
     213                 :            :  */
     214                 :            : static TimeLineID recoveryTargetTLI;
     215                 :            : List *expectedTLIs;
     216                 :            : static TimeLineID curFileTLI;
     217                 :            : 
     218                 :            : /*
     219                 :            :  * ProcLastRecPtr points to the start of the last XLOG record inserted by the
     220                 :            :  * current backend.  It is updated for all inserts.  XactLastRecEnd points to
     221                 :            :  * end+1 of the last record, and is reset when we end a top-level transaction,
     222                 :            :  * or start a new one; so it can be used to tell if the current transaction has
     223                 :            :  * created any XLOG records.
     224                 :            :  */
     225                 :            : static XLogRecPtr ProcLastRecPtr = {0, 0};
     226                 :            : 
     227                 :            : XLogRecPtr      XactLastRecEnd = {0, 0};
     228                 :            : 
     229                 :            : static uint32 ProcLastRecTotalLen = 0;
     230                 :            : 
     231                 :            : static uint32 ProcLastRecDataLen = 0;
     232                 :            : 
     233                 :            : /*
     234                 :            :  * RedoRecPtr is this backend's local copy of the REDO record pointer
     235                 :            :  * (which is almost but not quite the same as a pointer to the most recent
     236                 :            :  * CHECKPOINT record).  We update this from the shared-memory copy,
     237                 :            :  * XLogCtl->Insert.RedoRecPtr, whenever we can safely do so (ie, when we
     238                 :            :  * hold the Insert lock).  See XLogInsert for details.  We are also allowed
     239                 :            :  * to update from XLogCtl->Insert.RedoRecPtr if we hold the info_lck;
     240                 :            :  * see GetRedoRecPtr.  A freshly spawned backend obtains the value during
     241                 :            :  * InitXLOGAccess.
     242                 :            :  */
     243                 :            : static XLogRecPtr RedoRecPtr;
     244                 :            : 
     245                 :            : /*
     246                 :            :  * RedoStartLSN points to the checkpoint's REDO location which is specified
     247                 :            :  * in a backup label file, backup history file or control file. In standby
     248                 :            :  * mode, XLOG streaming usually starts from the position where an invalid
     249                 :            :  * record was found. But if we fail to read even the initial checkpoint
     250                 :            :  * record, we use the REDO location instead of the checkpoint location as
     251                 :            :  * the start position of XLOG streaming. Otherwise we would have to jump
     252                 :            :  * backwards to the REDO location after reading the checkpoint record,
     253                 :            :  * because the REDO record can precede the checkpoint record.
     254                 :            :  */
     255                 :            : static XLogRecPtr RedoStartLSN = {0, 0};
     256                 :            : 
     257                 :            : /*----------
     258                 :            :  * Shared-memory data structures for XLOG control
     259                 :            :  *
     260                 :            :  * LogwrtRqst indicates a byte position that we need to write and/or fsync
     261                 :            :  * the log up to (all records before that point must be written or fsynced).
     262                 :            :  * LogwrtResult indicates the byte positions we have already written/fsynced.
     263                 :            :  * These structs are identical but are declared separately to indicate their
     264                 :            :  * slightly different functions.
     265                 :            :  *
     266                 :            :  * We do a lot of pushups to minimize the amount of access to lockable
     267                 :            :  * shared memory values.  There are actually three shared-memory copies of
     268                 :            :  * LogwrtResult, plus one unshared copy in each backend.  Here's how it works:
     269                 :            :  *              XLogCtl->LogwrtResult is protected by info_lck
     270                 :            :  *              XLogCtl->Write.LogwrtResult is protected by WALWriteLock
     271                 :            :  *              XLogCtl->Insert.LogwrtResult is protected by WALInsertLock
     272                 :            :  * One must hold the associated lock to read or write any of these, but
     273                 :            :  * of course no lock is needed to read/write the unshared LogwrtResult.
     274                 :            :  *
     275                 :            :  * XLogCtl->LogwrtResult and XLogCtl->Write.LogwrtResult are both "always
     276                 :            :  * right", since both are updated by a write or flush operation before
     277                 :            :  * it releases WALWriteLock.  The point of keeping XLogCtl->Write.LogwrtResult
     278                 :            :  * is that it can be examined/modified by code that already holds WALWriteLock
     279                 :            :  * without needing to grab info_lck as well.
     280                 :            :  *
     281                 :            :  * XLogCtl->Insert.LogwrtResult may lag behind the reality of the other two,
     282                 :            :  * but is updated when convenient.      Again, it exists for the convenience of
     283                 :            :  * code that is already holding WALInsertLock but not the other locks.
     284                 :            :  *
     285                 :            :  * The unshared LogwrtResult may lag behind any or all of these, and again
     286                 :            :  * is updated when convenient.
     287                 :            :  *
     288                 :            :  * The request bookkeeping is simpler: there is a shared XLogCtl->LogwrtRqst
     289                 :            :  * (protected by info_lck), but we don't need to cache any copies of it.
     290                 :            :  *
     291                 :            :  * Note that this all works because the request and result positions can only
     292                 :            :  * advance forward, never back up, and so we can easily determine which of two
     293                 :            :  * values is "more up to date".
     294                 :            :  *
     295                 :            :  * info_lck is only held long enough to read/update the protected variables,
     296                 :            :  * so it's a plain spinlock.  The other locks are held longer (potentially
     297                 :            :  * over I/O operations), so we use LWLocks for them.  These locks are:
     298                 :            :  *
     299                 :            :  * WALInsertLock: must be held to insert a record into the WAL buffers.
     300                 :            :  *
     301                 :            :  * WALWriteLock: must be held to write WAL buffers to disk (XLogWrite or
     302                 :            :  * XLogFlush).
     303                 :            :  *
     304                 :            :  * ControlFileLock: must be held to read/update control file or create
     305                 :            :  * new log file.
     306                 :            :  *
     307                 :            :  * CheckpointLock: must be held to do a checkpoint (ensures only one
     308                 :            :  * checkpointer at a time; currently, with all checkpoints done by the
     309                 :            :  * bgwriter, this is just pro forma).
     310                 :            :  *
     311                 :            :  *----------
     312                 :            :  */
     313                 :            : 
     314                 :            : typedef struct XLogwrtRqst
     315                 :            : {
     316                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      Write;                  /* last byte + 1 to write out */
     317                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      Flush;                  /* last byte + 1 to flush */
     318                 :            : } XLogwrtRqst;
     319                 :            : 
     320                 :            : typedef struct XLogwrtResult
     321                 :            : {
     322                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      Write;                  /* last byte + 1 written out */
     323                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      Flush;                  /* last byte + 1 flushed */
     324                 :            : } XLogwrtResult;
     325                 :            : 
     326                 :            : /*
     327                 :            :  * Shared state data for XLogInsert.
     328                 :            :  */
     329                 :            : typedef struct XLogCtlInsert
     330                 :            : {
     331                 :            :         XLogwrtResult LogwrtResult; /* a recent value of LogwrtResult */
     332                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      PrevRecord;             /* start of previously-inserted record */
     333                 :            :         int                     curridx;                /* current block index in cache */
     334                 :            :         XLogPageHeader currpage;        /* points to header of block in cache */
     335                 :            :         char       *currpos;            /* current insertion point in cache */
     336                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      RedoRecPtr;             /* current redo point for insertions */
     337                 :            :         bool            forcePageWrites;        /* forcing full-page writes for PITR? */
     338                 :            : 
     339                 :            :         /*
     340                 :            :          * exclusiveBackup is true if a backup started with pg_start_backup() is
     341                 :            :          * in progress, and nonExclusiveBackups is a counter indicating the number
     342                 :            :          * of streaming base backups currently in progress. forcePageWrites is set
     343                 :            :          * to true when either of these is non-zero. lastBackupStart is the latest
     344                 :            :          * checkpoint redo location used as a starting point for an online backup.
     345                 :            :          */
     346                 :            :         bool            exclusiveBackup;
     347                 :            :         int                     nonExclusiveBackups;
     348                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      lastBackupStart;
     349                 :            : } XLogCtlInsert;
     350                 :            : 
     351                 :            : /*
     352                 :            :  * Shared state data for XLogWrite/XLogFlush.
     353                 :            :  */
     354                 :            : typedef struct XLogCtlWrite
     355                 :            : {
     356                 :            :         XLogwrtResult LogwrtResult; /* current value of LogwrtResult */
     357                 :            :         int                     curridx;                /* cache index of next block to write */
     358                 :            :         time_t          lastSegSwitchTime;              /* time of last xlog segment switch */
     359                 :            : } XLogCtlWrite;
     360                 :            : 
     361                 :            : /*
     362                 :            :  * Total shared-memory state for XLOG.
     363                 :            :  */
     364                 :            : typedef struct XLogCtlData
     365                 :            : {
     366                 :            :         /* Protected by WALInsertLock: */
     367                 :            :         XLogCtlInsert Insert;
     368                 :            : 
     369                 :            :         /* Protected by info_lck: */
     370                 :            :         XLogwrtRqst LogwrtRqst;
     371                 :            :         XLogwrtResult LogwrtResult;
     372                 :            :         uint32          ckptXidEpoch;   /* nextXID & epoch of latest checkpoint */
     373                 :            :         TransactionId ckptXid;
     374                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      asyncCommitLSN; /* LSN of newest async commit */
     375                 :            :         uint32          lastRemovedLog; /* latest removed/recycled XLOG segment */
     376                 :            :         uint32          lastRemovedSeg;
     377                 :            : 
     378                 :            :         /* Protected by WALWriteLock: */
     379                 :            :         XLogCtlWrite Write;
     380                 :            : 
     381                 :            :         /* Protected by ChangeTrackingTransitionLock. */
     382                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      lastChangeTrackingEndLoc;
     383                 :            :                                                                 /*
     384                 :            :                                                                  * End + 1 of the last XLOG record inserted and
     385                 :            :                                                                  * (possible) change tracked.
     386                 :            :                                                                  */
     387                 :            : 
     388                 :            :         /* Resynchronize */
     389                 :            :         bool            sendingResynchronizeTransitionMsg;
     390                 :            :         slock_t         resynchronize_lck;              /* locks shared variables shown above */
     391                 :            : 
     392                 :            :         /*
     393                 :            :          * These values do not change after startup, although the pointed-to pages
     394                 :            :          * and xlblocks values certainly do.  Permission to read/write the pages
     395                 :            :          * and xlblocks values depends on WALInsertLock and WALWriteLock.
     396                 :            :          */
     397                 :            :         char       *pages;                      /* buffers for unwritten XLOG pages */
     398                 :            :         XLogRecPtr *xlblocks;           /* 1st byte ptr-s + XLOG_BLCKSZ */
     399                 :            :         Size            XLogCacheByte;  /* # bytes in xlog buffers */
     400                 :            :         int                     XLogCacheBlck;  /* highest allocated xlog buffer index */
     401                 :            :         TimeLineID      ThisTimeLineID;
     402                 :            : 
     403                 :            :         /*
     404                 :            :          * SharedRecoveryInProgress indicates if we're still in crash or standby
     405                 :            :          * mode. Currently use of this variable is very limited for e.g. WAL receiver
     406                 :            :          * Protected by info_lck.
     407                 :            :          */
     408                 :            :         bool            SharedRecoveryInProgress;
     409                 :            : 
     410                 :            :         /*
     411                 :            :          * recoveryWakeupLatch is used to wake up the startup process to continue
     412                 :            :          * WAL replay, if it is waiting for WAL to arrive or failover trigger file
     413                 :            :          * to appear.
     414                 :            :          */
     415                 :            :         Latch           recoveryWakeupLatch;
     416                 :            : 
     417                 :            :         /*
     418                 :            :          * the standby's dbid when it runs.  Used in mmxlog to emit standby filepath.
     419                 :            :          * Protected by info_lck
     420                 :            :          */
     421                 :            :         int16           standbyDbid;
     422                 :            : 
     423                 :            :         slock_t         info_lck;               /* locks shared variables shown above */
     424                 :            : 
     425                 :            :         /*
     426                 :            :          * Save the location of the last checkpoint record to enable supressing
     427                 :            :          * unnecessary checkpoint records -- when no new xlog has been written
     428                 :            :          * since the last one.
     429                 :            :          */
     430                 :            :         bool            haveLastCheckpointLoc;
     431                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      lastCheckpointLoc;
     432                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      lastCheckpointEndLoc;
     433                 :            : 
     434                 :            :         /*
     435                 :            :          * lastReplayedEndRecPtr points to end+1 of the last record successfully
     436                 :            :          * replayed.
     437                 :            :          */
     438                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      lastReplayedEndRecPtr;
     439                 :            : 
     440                 :            :         /* current effective recovery target timeline */
     441                 :            :         TimeLineID      RecoveryTargetTLI;
     442                 :            : 
     443                 :            :         /*
     444                 :            :          * timestamp of when we started replaying the current chunk of WAL data,
     445                 :            :          * only relevant for replication or archive recovery
     446                 :            :          */
     447                 :            :         TimestampTz currentChunkStartTime;
     448                 :            : 
     449                 :            :         /*
     450                 :            :          * Save the redo range used in Pass 1 recovery so it can be used in subsequent passes.
     451                 :            :          */
     452                 :            :         bool            multipleRecoveryPassesNeeded;
     453                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      pass1StartLoc;
     454                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      pass1LastLoc;
     455                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      pass1LastCheckpointLoc;
     456                 :            : 
     457                 :            :         /*=================Pass 4 PersistentTable-Cat verification================*/
     458                 :            :         /*If true integrity checks will be performed in Pass4.*/
     459                 :            :         bool            integrityCheckNeeded;
     460                 :            : 
     461                 :            :         /*
     462                 :            :          * Currently set database and tablespace to be verified for database specific
     463                 :            :          * PT-Cat verification in Pass4. These fields also act as implicit flags
     464                 :            :          * PT-Cat which indicate if there are any more databases to perform
     465                 :            :          * PT-Cat verifications checks on.
     466                 :            :          */
     467                 :            :         Oid                     currentDatabaseToVerify;
     468                 :            :         Oid                     tablespaceOfCurrentDatabaseToVerify;
     469                 :            : 
     470                 :            :         /*Indicates if pass4 PT-Cat verification checks passed*/
     471                 :            :         bool            pass4_PTCatVerificationPassed;
     472                 :            :         /*==========Pass 4 PersistentTable-Cat verification End===================*/
     473                 :            : 
     474                 :            : } XLogCtlData;
     475                 :            : 
     476                 :            : static XLogCtlData *XLogCtl = NULL;
     477                 :            : 
     478                 :            : /*
     479                 :            :  * We maintain an image of pg_control in shared memory.
     480                 :            :  */
     481                 :            : static ControlFileData *ControlFile = NULL;
     482                 :            : 
     483                 :            : typedef struct ControlFileWatch
     484                 :            : {
     485                 :            :         bool            watcherInitialized;
     486                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      current_checkPointLoc;          /* current last check point record ptr */
     487                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      current_prevCheckPointLoc;  /* current previous check point record ptr */
     488                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      current_checkPointCopy_redo;
     489                 :            :                                                                 /* current checkpointCopy value for
     490                 :            :                                                                  * next RecPtr available when we began to
     491                 :            :                                                                  * create CheckPoint (i.e. REDO start point) */
     492                 :            : 
     493                 :            : } ControlFileWatch;
     494                 :            : 
     495                 :            : 
     496                 :            : /*
     497                 :            :  * We keep the watcher in shared memory.
     498                 :            :  */
     499                 :            : static ControlFileWatch *ControlFileWatcher = NULL;
     500                 :            : 
     501                 :            : /*
     502                 :            :  * Macros for managing XLogInsert state.  In most cases, the calling routine
     503                 :            :  * has local copies of XLogCtl->Insert and/or XLogCtl->Insert->curridx,
     504                 :            :  * so these are passed as parameters instead of being fetched via XLogCtl.
     505                 :            :  */
     506                 :            : 
     507                 :            : /* Free space remaining in the current xlog page buffer */
     508                 :            : #define INSERT_FREESPACE(Insert)  \
     509                 :            :         (XLOG_BLCKSZ - ((Insert)->currpos - (char *) (Insert)->currpage))
     510                 :            : 
     511                 :            : /* Construct XLogRecPtr value for current insertion point */
     512                 :            : #define INSERT_RECPTR(recptr,Insert,curridx)  \
     513                 :            :         ( \
     514                 :            :           (recptr).xlogid = XLogCtl->xlblocks[curridx].xlogid, \
     515                 :            :           (recptr).xrecoff = \
     516                 :            :                 XLogCtl->xlblocks[curridx].xrecoff - INSERT_FREESPACE(Insert) \
     517                 :            :         )
     518                 :            : 
     519                 :            : #define PrevBufIdx(idx)         \
     520                 :            :                 (((idx) == 0) ? XLogCtl->XLogCacheBlck : ((idx) - 1))
     521                 :            : 
     522                 :            : #define NextBufIdx(idx)         \
     523                 :            :                 (((idx) == XLogCtl->XLogCacheBlck) ? 0 : ((idx) + 1))
     524                 :            : 
     525                 :            : /*
     526                 :            :  * Private, possibly out-of-date copy of shared LogwrtResult.
     527                 :            :  * See discussion above.
     528                 :            :  */
     529                 :            : static XLogwrtResult LogwrtResult = {{0, 0}, {0, 0}};
     530                 :            : 
     531                 :            : /*
     532                 :            :  * Codes indicating where we got a WAL file from during recovery, or where
     533                 :            :  * to attempt to get one.  These are chosen so that they can be OR'd together
     534                 :            :  * in a bitmask state variable.
     535                 :            :  */
     536                 :            : #define XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE               (1<<0)    /* Restored using restore_command */
     537                 :            : #define XLOG_FROM_PG_XLOG               (1<<1)    /* Existing file in pg_xlog */
     538                 :            : #define XLOG_FROM_STREAM                (1<<2)    /* Streamed from master */
     539                 :            : 
     540                 :            : /*
     541                 :            :  * openLogFile is -1 or a kernel FD for an open log file segment.
     542                 :            :  * When it's open, openLogOff is the current seek offset in the file.
     543                 :            :  * openLogId/openLogSeg identify the segment.  These variables are only
     544                 :            :  * used to write the XLOG, and so will normally refer to the active segment.
     545                 :            :  */
     546                 :            : static MirroredFlatFileOpen     mirroredLogFileOpen = MirroredFlatFileOpen_Init;
     547                 :            : static uint32 openLogId = 0;
     548                 :            : static uint32 openLogSeg = 0;
     549                 :            : static uint32 openLogOff = 0;
     550                 :            : 
     551                 :            : /*
     552                 :            :  * These variables are used similarly to the ones above, but for reading
     553                 :            :  * the XLOG.  Note, however, that readOff generally represents the offset
     554                 :            :  * of the page just read, not the seek position of the FD itself, which
     555                 :            :  * will be just past that page.readLen indicates how much of the current
     556                 :            :  * page has been read into readBuf, and readSource indicates where we got
     557                 :            :  * the currently open file from.
     558                 :            :  */
     559                 :            : static int      readFile = -1;
     560                 :            : static uint32 readId = 0;
     561                 :            : static uint32 readSeg = 0;
     562                 :            : static uint32 readOff = 0;
     563                 :            : static uint32 readLen = 0;
     564                 :            : static int      readSource = 0;         /* XLOG_FROM_* code */
     565                 :            : 
     566                 :            : /*
     567                 :            :  * Keeps track of which sources we've tried to read the current WAL
     568                 :            :  * record from and failed.
     569                 :            :  */
     570                 :            : static int      failedSources = 0;      /* OR of XLOG_FROM_* codes */
     571                 :            : 
     572                 :            : /*
     573                 :            :  * These variables track when we last obtained some WAL data to process,
     574                 :            :  * and where we got it from.  (XLogReceiptSource is initially the same as
     575                 :            :  * readSource, but readSource gets reset to zero when we don't have data
     576                 :            :  * to process right now.)
     577                 :            :  */
     578                 :            : static TimestampTz XLogReceiptTime = 0;
     579                 :            : static int      XLogReceiptSource = 0;          /* XLOG_FROM_* code */
     580                 :            : 
     581                 :            : /* Buffer for currently read page (XLOG_BLCKSZ bytes) */
     582                 :            : static char *readBuf = NULL;
     583                 :            : 
     584                 :            : /* Buffer for current ReadRecord result (expandable) */
     585                 :            : static char *readRecordBuf = NULL;
     586                 :            : static uint32 readRecordBufSize = 0;
     587                 :            : 
     588                 :            : /* State information for XLOG reading */
     589                 :            : static XLogRecPtr ReadRecPtr;   /* start of last record read */
     590                 :            : static XLogRecPtr EndRecPtr;    /* end+1 of last record read */
     591                 :            : static XLogRecord *nextRecord = NULL;
     592                 :            : static TimeLineID lastPageTLI = 0;
     593                 :            : static TimeLineID lastSegmentTLI = 0;
     594                 :            : 
     595                 :            : static bool InRedo = false;
     596                 :            : 
     597                 :            : /* Aligning xlog mirrored buffer */
     598                 :            : static int32 writeBufLen = 0;
     599                 :            : static char     *writeBuf = NULL;
     600                 :            : 
     601                 :            : char    *writeBufAligned = NULL;
     602                 :            : 
     603                 :            : /*
     604                 :            :  * Flag set by interrupt handlers for later service in the redo loop.
     605                 :            :  */
     606                 :            : static volatile sig_atomic_t got_SIGHUP = false;

     608                 :            :

     612                 :            :  */

     714                 :            : 
     715                 :            : /*
     716                 :            :  * Whether we need to always generate transaction log (XLOG), or if we can
     717                 :            :  * bypass it and get better performance.
     718                 :            :  *
     719                 :            :  * For GPDB, we currently do not support XLogArchivingActive(), so we don't
     720                 :            :  * use it as a condition.
     721                 :            :  */
     722                 :            : bool XLog_CanBypassWal(void)
     723                 :            : {
     724                 :            : #ifdef USE_SEGWALREP
     725                 :            :         /*
     726                 :            :          * Wal replication enabled for segments, shouldn't skip anything from
     727                 :            :          * wal.
     728                 :            :          */
     729                 :            :         return false;
     730                 :            : #else
     731                 :            :         /*
     732                 :            :          * We need the XLOG to be transmitted to the standby master since it is
     733                 :            :          * not using FileRep technology yet. Master also could skip some of the
     734                 :            :          * WAL operations for optimization when standby is not configured, but for
     735                 :            :          * now we lean towards safety.
     736                 :            :          */
     737                 :      18193 :         return GpIdentity.segindex != MASTER_CONTENT_ID;
     738                 :            : #endif
     739                 :            : }
     740                 :            : 
     741                 :            : /*
     742                 :            :  * For FileRep code that doesn't have the Bypass WAL logic yet.

     744                 :            : bool XLog_UnconvertedCanBypassWal(void)
     745                 :            : {
     746                 :      58607 :         return false;
     747                 :            : }

     749                 :            : static char *XLogContiguousCopy(
     750                 :            :         XLogRecord              *record,
     751                 :            : 
     752                 :            :         XLogRecData     *rdata)
     753                 :            : {
     754                 :            :         XLogRecData *rdt;
     755                 :            :         int32 len;
     756                 :            :         char *buffer;
     757                 :            : 
     758                 :          0 :         rdt = rdata;
     759                 :          0 :         len = SizeOfXLogRecord;
     760         [ #  # ]:          0 :         while (rdt != NULL)
     761                 :            :         {
     762         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (rdt->data != NULL)
     763                 :            :                 {
     764                 :          0 :                         len += rdt->len;
     765                 :          0 :                 }
     766                 :          0 :                 rdt = rdt->next;
     767                 :            :         }
     768                 :            : 
     769                 :          0 :         buffer = (char*)palloc(len);
     770                 :            : 
     771                 :          0 :         memcpy(buffer, record, SizeOfXLogRecord);
     772                 :          0 :         rdt = rdata;
     773                 :          0 :         len = SizeOfXLogRecord;
     774         [ #  # ]:          0 :         while (rdt != NULL)
     775                 :            :         {
     776         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (rdt->data != NULL)
     777                 :            :                 {
     778                 :          0 :                         memcpy(&buffer[len], rdt->data, rdt->len);
     779                 :          0 :                         len += rdt->len;
     780                 :          0 :                 }
     781                 :          0 :                 rdt = rdt->next;
     782                 :            :         }
     783                 :            : 
     784                 :          0 :         return buffer;
     785                 :            : }
     786                 :            : 
     787                 :            : /*
     788                 :            :  * Insert an XLOG record having the specified RMID and info bytes,
     789                 :            :  * with the body of the record being the data chunk(s) described by
     790                 :            :  * the rdata chain (see xlog.h for notes about rdata).
     791                 :            :  *
     792                 :            :  * Returns XLOG pointer to end of record (beginning of next record).
     793                 :            :  * This can be used as LSN for data pages affected by the logged action.
     794                 :            :  * (LSN is the XLOG point up to which the XLOG must be flushed to disk
     795                 :            :  * before the data page can be written out.  This implements the basic
     796                 :            :  * WAL rule "write the log before the data".)
     797                 :            :  *
     798                 :            :  * NB: this routine feels free to scribble on the XLogRecData structs,
     799                 :            :  * though not on the data they reference.  This is OK since the XLogRecData
     800                 :            :  * structs are always just temporaries in the calling code.
     801                 :            :  */
     802                 :            : XLogRecPtr
     803                 :            : XLogInsert(RmgrId rmid, uint8 info, XLogRecData *rdata)
     804                 :            : {
     805                 :   63816936 :         return XLogInsert_Internal(rmid, info, rdata, GetCurrentTransactionIdIfAny());
     806                 :            : }
     807                 :            : 
     808                 :            : XLogRecPtr
     809                 :            : XLogInsert_OverrideXid(RmgrId rmid, uint8 info, XLogRecData *rdata, TransactionId overrideXid)
     810                 :            : {
     811                 :    3091437 :         return XLogInsert_Internal(rmid, info, rdata, overrideXid);
     812                 :            : }
     813                 :            : 
     814                 :            : 
     815                 :            : static XLogRecPtr
     816                 :            : XLogInsert_Internal(RmgrId rmid, uint8 info, XLogRecData *rdata, TransactionId headerXid)
     817                 :            : {
     818                 :            : 
     819                 :   66908373 :         XLogCtlInsert *Insert = &XLogCtl->Insert;
     820                 :            :         XLogRecord *record;
     821                 :            :         XLogContRecord *contrecord;
     822                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      RecPtr;
     823                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      WriteRqst;
     824                 :            :         uint32          freespace;
     825                 :            :         int                     curridx;
     826                 :            :         XLogRecData *rdt;
     827                 :   66908373 :         char            *rdatabuf = NULL;
     828                 :            :         Buffer          dtbuf[XLR_MAX_BKP_BLOCKS];
     829                 :            :         bool            dtbuf_bkp[XLR_MAX_BKP_BLOCKS];
     830                 :            :         BkpBlock        dtbuf_xlg[XLR_MAX_BKP_BLOCKS];
     831                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      dtbuf_lsn[XLR_MAX_BKP_BLOCKS];
     832                 :            :         XLogRecData dtbuf_rdt1[XLR_MAX_BKP_BLOCKS];
     833                 :            :         XLogRecData dtbuf_rdt2[XLR_MAX_BKP_BLOCKS];
     834                 :            :         XLogRecData dtbuf_rdt3[XLR_MAX_BKP_BLOCKS];
     835                 :            :         pg_crc32        rdata_crc;
     836                 :            :         uint32          len,
     837                 :            :                                 write_len;
     838                 :            :         unsigned        i;
     839                 :            :         bool            updrqst;
     840                 :            :         bool            doPageWrites;
     841         [ +  + ]:   66908373 :         bool            isLogSwitch = (rmid == RM_XLOG_ID && info == XLOG_SWITCH);
     842                 :            : 
     843                 :   66908373 :         bool            rdata_iscopy = false;
     844                 :            : 
     845                 :            :     /* Safety check in case our assumption is ever broken. */
     846                 :            :         /* NOTE: This is slightly modified from the one in xact.c -- the test for */
     847                 :            :         /* NOTE: seqXlogWrite is omitted... */
     848                 :            :         /* NOTE: some local-only changes are OK */
     849 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:   66908373 :         if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_EXECUTE && !Gp_is_writer)
     850                 :            :         {
     851                 :            :                 /*
     852                 :            :              * we better only do really minor things on the reader that result
     853                 :            :              * in writing to the xlog here at commit.  for now sequences
     854                 :            :              * should be the only one
     855                 :            :              */
     856         [ -  + ]:         18 :                 if (DistributedTransactionContext == DTX_CONTEXT_LOCAL_ONLY)
     857                 :            :                 {
     858                 :            :                         /* MPP-1687: readers may under some circumstances extend the CLOG
     859                 :            :                          * rmid == RM_CLOG_ID and info having CLOG_ZEROPAGE set */
     860                 :          0 :                         elog(LOG, "Reader qExec committing LOCAL_ONLY changes. (%d %d)", rmid, info);
     861                 :          0 :                 }
     862                 :            :                 else
     863                 :            :                 {
     864                 :            :                         /*
     865                 :            :                          * We are allowing the QE Reader to write to support error tables.
     866                 :            :                          */
     867                 :         18 :                         elog(DEBUG1, "Reader qExec writing changes. (%d %d)", rmid, info);
     868                 :            : #ifdef nothing
     869                 :            :                         ereport(ERROR,
     870                 :            :                                         (errmsg("Reader qExec had local changes to commit! (rmid = %u)",
     871                 :            :                                                         rmid),
     872                 :            :                                          errdetail("A Reader qExec tried to commit local changes.  "
     873                 :            :                                                            "Only the single Writer qExec can do so. "),
     874                 :            :                                          errhint("This is most likely the result of a feature being turned "
     875                 :            :                                                          "on that violates the single WRITER principle")));
     876                 :            : #endif
     877                 :            :                 }
     878                 :         18 :         }
     879                 :            : 
     880                 :            :         /* info's high bits are reserved for use by me */
     881         [ -  + ]:   66908373 :         if (info & XLR_INFO_MASK)
     882                 :          0 :                 elog(PANIC, "invalid xlog info mask %02X", info);
     883                 :            : 
     884                 :            :         /*
     885                 :            :          * In bootstrap mode, we don't actually log anything but XLOG resources;
     886                 :            :          * return a phony record pointer.
     887                 :            :          */
     888 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:   66908373 :         if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode() && rmid != RM_XLOG_ID)
     889                 :            :         {
     890                 :          0 :                 RecPtr.xlogid = 0;
     891                 :          0 :                 RecPtr.xrecoff = SizeOfXLogLongPHD;             /* start of 1st chkpt record */
     892                 :          0 :                 return RecPtr;
     893                 :            :         }
     894                 :            : 
     895                 :            :         /*
     896                 :            :          * Here we scan the rdata chain, determine which buffers must be backed
     897                 :            :          * up, and compute the CRC values for the data.  Note that the record
     898                 :            :          * header isn't added into the CRC initially since we don't know the final
     899                 :            :          * length or info bits quite yet.  Thus, the CRC will represent the CRC of
     900                 :            :          * the whole record in the order "rdata, then backup blocks, then record
     901                 :            :          * header".
     902                 :            :          *
     903                 :            :          * We may have to loop back to here if a race condition is detected below.
     904                 :            :          * We could prevent the race by doing all this work while holding the
     905                 :            :          * insert lock, but it seems better to avoid doing CRC calculations while
     906                 :            :          * holding the lock.  This means we have to be careful about modifying the
     907                 :            :          * rdata chain until we know we aren't going to loop back again.  The only
     908                 :            :          * change we allow ourselves to make earlier is to set rdt->data = NULL in
     909                 :            :          * chain items we have decided we will have to back up the whole buffer
     910                 :            :          * for.  This is OK because we will certainly decide the same thing again
     911                 :            :          * for those items if we do it over; doing it here saves an extra pass
     912                 :            :          * over the chain later.
     913                 :            :          */

     916                 :            :         {
     917                 :  200726238 :                 dtbuf[i] = InvalidBuffer;
     918                 :  200726238 :                 dtbuf_bkp[i] = false;
     919                 :  200726238 :         }
     920                 :            : 
     921                 :            :         /*
     922                 :            :          * Decide if we need to do full-page writes in this XLOG record: true if

     924                 :            :          * don't yet have the insert lock, forcePageWrites could change under us,

     937                 :   70534287 :                         COMP_CRC32C(rdata_crc, rdt->data, rdt->len);
     938                 :   70534287 :                 }

     940                 :            :                 {

     950                 :            :                                         {
     951                 :   35418119 :                                                 len += rdt->len;
     952                 :   35418119 :                                                 COMP_CRC32C(rdata_crc, rdt->data, rdt->len);
     953                 :   35418119 :                                         }
     954                 :   35437335 :                                         break;
     955                 :            :                                 }
     956         [ +  + ]:   62307924 :                                 if (dtbuf[i] == InvalidBuffer)

     958                 :            :                                         /* OK, put it in this slot */
     959                 :   62129721 :                                         dtbuf[i] = rdt->buffer;
     960 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:   62129721 :                                         if (XLogCheckBuffer(rdt, doPageWrites,

     964                 :      34040 :                                                 rdt->data = NULL;

     966         [ +  + ]:   62095681 :                                         else if (rdt->data)
     967                 :            :                                         {
     968                 :   56730428 :                                                 len += rdt->len;

    1001                 :          6 :                         }
    1002                 :            :                         else
    1003                 :            :                         {
    1004                 :            :                                 /* must skip the hole */

    1029                 :            :          * Check to see if my RedoRecPtr is out of date.  If so, may have to go
    1030                 :            :          * back and recompute everything.  This can only happen just after a
    1031                 :            :          * checkpoint, so it's better to be slow in this case and fast otherwise.
    1032                 :            :          *

    1078                 :            :         /*

    1310                 :            :          * existing data, and then forcibly advance to the start of the next
    1311                 :            :          * segment.  It's not good to do this I/O while holding the insert lock,
    1312                 :            :          * but there seems too much risk of confusion if we try to release the

    1578                 :            : /*
    1579                 :            :  * XLogArchiveNotify
    1580                 :            :  *
    1581                 :            :  * Create an archive notification file

    1583                 :            :  * The name of the notification file is the message that will be picked up
    1584                 :            :  * by the archiver, e.g. we write 0000000100000001000000C6.ready
    1585                 :            :  * and the archiver then knows to archive XLOGDIR/0000000100000001000000C6,

    1598                 :            :         {
    1599         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(LOG,
    1600                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    1601                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not create archive status file \"%s\": %m",
    1602                 :            :                                                 archiveStatusPath)));

    1604                 :            :         }

    1606                 :            :         {
    1607         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(LOG,

    1609                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not write archive status file \"%s\": %m",
    1610                 :            :                                                 archiveStatusPath)));
    1611                 :          0 :                 return;
    1612                 :            :         }

    1614                 :            :         /* Notify archiver that it's got something to do */

    1631                 :            : /*
    1632                 :            :  * XLogArchiveCheckDone
    1633                 :            :  *
    1634                 :            :  * This is called when we are ready to delete or recycle an old XLOG segment
    1635                 :            :  * file or backup history file.  If it is okay to delete it then return true.
    1636                 :            :  * If it is not time to delete it, make sure a .ready file exists, and return
    1637                 :            :  * false.
    1638                 :            :  *
    1639                 :            :  * If <XLOG>.done exists, then return true; else if <XLOG>.ready exists,
    1640                 :            :  * then return false; else create <XLOG>.ready and return false.
    1641                 :            :  *
    1642                 :            :  * The reason we do things this way is so that if the original attempt to
    1643                 :            :  * create <XLOG>.ready fails, we'll retry during subsequent checkpoints.
    1644                 :            :  */
    1645                 :            : static bool
    1646                 :            : XLogArchiveCheckDone(const char *xlog)
    1647                 :            : {
    1648                 :            :         char            archiveStatusPath[MAXPGPATH];
    1649                 :            :         struct stat stat_buf;
    1650                 :            : 
    1651                 :            :         /* Always deletable if archiving is off */
    1652         [ +  - ]:          7 :         if (!XLogArchivingActive())
    1653                 :          7 :                 return true;
    1654                 :            : 
    1655                 :            :         /* First check for .done --- this means archiver is done with it */
    1656                 :          0 :         StatusFilePath(archiveStatusPath, xlog, ".done");
    1657         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (stat(archiveStatusPath, &stat_buf) == 0)
    1658                 :          0 :                 return true;
    1659                 :            : 
    1660                 :            :         /* check for .ready --- this means archiver is still busy with it */
    1661                 :          0 :         StatusFilePath(archiveStatusPath, xlog, ".ready");
    1662         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (stat(archiveStatusPath, &stat_buf) == 0)
    1663                 :          0 :                 return false;
    1664                 :            : 
    1665                 :            :         /* Race condition --- maybe archiver just finished, so recheck */
    1666                 :          0 :         StatusFilePath(archiveStatusPath, xlog, ".done");
    1667         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (stat(archiveStatusPath, &stat_buf) == 0)
    1668                 :          0 :                 return true;
    1669                 :            : 
    1670                 :            :         /* Retry creation of the .ready file */
    1671                 :          0 :         XLogArchiveNotify(xlog);
    1672                 :          0 :         return false;
    1673                 :          7 : }

    1675                 :            : /*
    1676                 :            :  * XLogArchiveCleanup

    1681                 :            : XLogArchiveCleanup(const char *xlog)

    1695                 :            : 
    1696                 :            : /*
    1697                 :            :  * Advance the Insert state to the next buffer page, writing out the next
    1698                 :            :  * buffer if it still contains unwritten data.
    1699                 :            :  *
    1700                 :            :  * If new_segment is TRUE then we set up the next buffer page as the first
    1701                 :            :  * page of the next xlog segment file, possibly but not usually the next
    1702                 :            :  * consecutive file page.
    1703                 :            :  *
    1704                 :            :  * The global LogwrtRqst.Write pointer needs to be advanced to include the
    1705                 :            :  * just-filled page.  If we can do this for free (without an extra lock),
    1706                 :            :  * we do so here.  Otherwise the caller must do it.  We return TRUE if the
    1707                 :            :  * request update still needs to be done, FALSE if we did it internally.
    1708                 :            :  *
    1709                 :            :  * Must be called with WALInsertLock held.
    1710                 :            :  */
    1711                 :            : static bool
    1712                 :            : AdvanceXLInsertBuffer(bool new_segment)
    1713                 :            : {
    1714                 :     313370 :         XLogCtlInsert *Insert = &XLogCtl->Insert;
    1715                 :     313370 :         XLogCtlWrite *Write = &XLogCtl->Write;
    1716         [ +  + ]:     313370 :         int                     nextidx = NextBufIdx(Insert->curridx);
    1717                 :     313370 :         bool            update_needed = true;
    1718                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      OldPageRqstPtr;
    1719                 :            :         XLogwrtRqst WriteRqst;
    1720                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      NewPageEndPtr;
    1721                 :            :         XLogPageHeader NewPage;
    1722                 :            : 
    1723                 :            :         /* Use Insert->LogwrtResult copy if it's more fresh */
    1724 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     313370 :         if (XLByteLT(LogwrtResult.Write, Insert->LogwrtResult.Write))
                 [ +  + ]
    1725                 :       3837 :                 LogwrtResult = Insert->LogwrtResult;
    1726                 :            : 
    1727                 :            :         /*
    1728                 :            :          * Get ending-offset of the buffer page we need to replace (this may be
    1729                 :            :          * zero if the buffer hasn't been used yet).  Fall through if it's already
    1730                 :            :          * written out.
    1731                 :            :          */

                 [ +  + ]

                 [ +  + ]
    1755                 :            :                 {

    1769                 :        236 :                         }

    1848                 :            : 
    1849                 :            : /*
    1850                 :            :  * Check whether we've consumed enough xlog space that a checkpoint is needed.
    1851                 :            :  *
    1852                 :            :  * Caller must have just finished filling the open log file (so that
    1853                 :            :  * openLogId/openLogSeg are valid).  We measure the distance from RedoRecPtr
    1854                 :            :  * to the open log file and see if that exceeds CheckPointSegments.
    1855                 :            :  *
    1856                 :            :  * Note: it is caller's responsibility that RedoRecPtr is up-to-date.
    1857                 :            :  */
    1858                 :            : static bool
    1859                 :            : XLogCheckpointNeeded(void)
    1860                 :            : {
    1861                 :            :         /*
    1862                 :            :          * A straight computation of segment number could overflow 32 bits. Rather
    1863                 :            :          * than assuming we have working 64-bit arithmetic, we compare the
    1864                 :            :          * highest-order bits separately, and force a checkpoint immediately when
    1865                 :            :          * they change.
    1866                 :            :          */
    1867                 :            :         uint32          old_segno,
    1868                 :            :                                 new_segno;
    1869                 :            :         uint32          old_highbits,
    1870                 :            :                                 new_highbits;
    1871                 :            : 
    1872                 :        127 :         old_segno = (RedoRecPtr.xlogid % XLogSegSize) * XLogSegsPerFile +
    1873                 :        127 :                 (RedoRecPtr.xrecoff / XLogSegSize);
    1874                 :        127 :         old_highbits = RedoRecPtr.xlogid / XLogSegSize;
    1875                 :        127 :         new_segno = (openLogId % XLogSegSize) * XLogSegsPerFile + openLogSeg;
    1876                 :        127 :         new_highbits = openLogId / XLogSegSize;
    1877 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        127 :         if (new_highbits != old_highbits ||
    1878                 :        127 :                 new_segno >= old_segno + (uint32) (CheckPointSegments - 1))
    1879                 :          0 :                 return true;
    1880                 :        127 :         return false;
    1881                 :        127 : }
    1882                 :            : 
    1883                 :            : /*
    1884                 :            :  * Write and/or fsync the log at least as far as WriteRqst indicates.
    1885                 :            :  *
    1886                 :            :  * If flexible == TRUE, we don't have to write as far as WriteRqst, but
    1887                 :            :  * may stop at any convenient boundary (such as a cache or logfile boundary).
    1888                 :            :  * This option allows us to avoid uselessly issuing multiple writes when a
    1889                 :            :  * single one would do.
    1890                 :            :  *
    1891                 :            :  * If xlog_switch == TRUE, we are intending an xlog segment switch, so
    1892                 :            :  * perform end-of-segment actions after writing the last page, even if
    1893                 :            :  * it's not physically the end of its segment.  (NB: this will work properly
    1894                 :            :  * only if caller specifies WriteRqst == page-end and flexible == false,
    1895                 :            :  * and there is some data to write.)
    1896                 :            :  *
    1897                 :            :  * Must be called with WALWriteLock held.
    1898                 :            :  */
    1899                 :            : static void
    1900                 :            : XLogWrite(XLogwrtRqst WriteRqst, bool flexible, bool xlog_switch)
    1901                 :            : {

    1903                 :            :         bool            ispartialpage;
    1904                 :            :         bool            last_iteration;
    1905                 :            :         bool            finishing_seg;
    1906                 :            :         bool            use_existent;
    1907                 :            :         int                     curridx;
    1908                 :            :         int                     npages;
    1909                 :            :         int                     startidx;
    1910                 :            :         uint32          startoffset;
    1911                 :            : 
    1912                 :            :         /* We should always be inside a critical section here */
    1913 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     496514 :         Assert(CritSectionCount > 0);
    1914                 :            : 
    1915                 :            :         /*
    1916                 :            :          * Update local LogwrtResult (caller probably did this already, but...)
    1917                 :            :          */
    1918                 :     496514 :         LogwrtResult = Write->LogwrtResult;
    1919                 :            : 
    1920                 :            :         /*
    1921                 :            :          * Since successive pages in the xlog cache are consecutively allocated,
    1922                 :            :          * we can usually gather multiple pages together and issue just one
    1923                 :            :          * write() call.  npages is the number of pages we have determined can be
    1924                 :            :          * written together; startidx is the cache block index of the first one,
    1925                 :            :          * and startoffset is the file offset at which it should go. The latter
    1926                 :            :          * two variables are only valid when npages > 0, but we must initialize
    1927                 :            :          * all of them to keep the compiler quiet.
    1928                 :            :          */

    1930                 :     496514 :         startidx = 0;
    1931                 :     496514 :         startoffset = 0;
    1932                 :            : 
    1933                 :            :         /*
    1934                 :            :          * Within the loop, curridx is the cache block index of the page to
    1935                 :            :          * consider writing.  We advance Write->curridx only after successfully
    1936                 :            :          * writing pages.  (Right now, this refinement is useless since we are
    1937                 :            :          * going to PANIC if any error occurs anyway; but someday it may come in

    1979                 :            : 
    1980                 :            :                 /* Make sure we have the current logfile open */

    2067                 :            : 
    2068                 :            :                         /*
    2069                 :            :                          * If we just wrote the whole last page of a logfile segment,
    2070                 :            :                          * fsync the segment immediately.  This avoids having to go back
    2071                 :            :                          * and re-open prior segments when an fsync request comes along
    2072                 :            :                          * later. Doing it here ensures that one and only one backend will
    2073                 :            :                          * perform this fsync.
    2074                 :            :                          *
    2075                 :            :                          * We also do this if this is the last page written for an xlog
    2076                 :            :                          * switch.
    2077                 :            :                          *
    2078                 :            :                          * This is also the right place to notify the Archiver that the
    2079                 :            :                          * segment is ready to copy to archival storage, and to update the
    2080                 :            :                          * timer for archive_timeout, and to signal for a checkpoint if
    2081                 :            :                          * too many logfile segments have been used since the last

    2085                 :            :                         {
    2086         [ +  - ]:        127 :                                 if (MirroredFlatFile_IsActive(&mirroredLogFileOpen))

    2088                 :            :                                                                         &mirroredLogFileOpen,
    2089                 :            :                                                                         /* suppressError */ false);
    2090                 :            : 
    2091                 :        127 :                                 elog((Debug_print_qd_mirroring ? LOG : DEBUG5),
    2092                 :            :                                          "XLogWrite (#1): flush loc %s; write loc %s",
    2093                 :            :                                          XLogLocationToString_Long(&LogwrtResult.Flush),
    2094                 :            :                                          XLogLocationToString2_Long(&LogwrtResult.Write));
    2095                 :            : 
    2096                 :        127 :                                 LogwrtResult.Flush = LogwrtResult.Write;                /* end of page */
    2097                 :            : 
    2098         [ -  + ]:        127 :                                 if (XLogArchivingActive())
    2099                 :          0 :                                         XLogArchiveNotifySeg(openLogId, openLogSeg);
    2100                 :            : 
    2101                 :        127 :                                 Write->lastSegSwitchTime = time(NULL);
    2102                 :            : 
    2103                 :            :                                 /*
    2104                 :            :                                  * Signal bgwriter to start a checkpoint if we've consumed too

    2106                 :            :                                  * using the local copy of RedoRecPtr, which might be out of

    2113         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (Debug_print_qd_mirroring)

    2116         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (XLogCheckpointNeeded())

    2119                 :        127 :                         }
    2120                 :     511197 :                 }
