    2312                 :            :         /*
    2313                 :            :          * If we still haven't flushed to the request point then we have a
    2314                 :            :          * problem; most likely, the requested flush point is past end of XLOG.
    2315                 :            :          * This has been seen to occur when a disk page has a corrupted LSN.
    2316                 :            :          *
    2317                 :            :          * Formerly we treated this as a PANIC condition, but that hurts the
    2318                 :            :          * system's robustness rather than helping it: we do not want to take down
    2319                 :            :          * the whole system due to corruption on one data page.  In particular, if
    2320                 :            :          * the bad page is encountered again during recovery then we would be
    2321                 :            :          * unable to restart the database at all!  (This scenario has actually
    2322                 :            :          * happened in the field several times with 7.1 releases. Note that we
    2323                 :            :          * cannot get here while InRedo is true, but if the bad page is brought in
    2324                 :            :          * and marked dirty during recovery then CreateCheckPoint will try to
    2325                 :            :          * flush it at the end of recovery.)
    2326                 :            :          *
    2327                 :            :          * The current approach is to ERROR under normal conditions, but only
    2328                 :            :          * WARNING during recovery, so that the system can be brought up even if
    2329                 :            :          * there's a corrupt LSN.  Note that for calls from xact.c, the ERROR will
    2330                 :            :          * be promoted to PANIC since xact.c calls this routine inside a critical
    2331                 :            :          * section.  However, calls from bufmgr.c are not within critical sections

                 [ -  + ]

    2355                 :            : /*
    2356                 :            :  * Flush xlog, but without specifying exactly where to flush to.
    2357                 :            :  *
    2358                 :            :  * We normally flush only completed blocks; but if there is nothing to do on
    2359                 :            :  * that basis, we check for unflushed async commits in the current incomplete
    2360                 :            :  * block, and flush through the latest one of those.  Thus, if async commits
    2361                 :            :  * are not being used, we will flush complete blocks only.      We can guarantee
    2362                 :            :  * that async commits reach disk after at most three cycles; normally only
    2363                 :            :  * one or two.  (We allow XLogWrite to write "flexibly", meaning it can stop
    2364                 :            :  * at the end of the buffer ring; this makes a difference only with very high
    2365                 :            :  * load or long wal_writer_delay, but imposes one extra cycle for the worst
    2366                 :            :  * case for async commits.)

    2368                 :            :  * This routine is invoked periodically by the background walwriter process.
    2369                 :            :  */

    2384                 :      50654 :                 SpinLockRelease(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    2385                 :            :         }
    2386                 :            : 
    2387                 :            :         /* back off to last completed page boundary */

    2389                 :            :

    2391 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:      50654 :         if (XLByteLE(WriteRqstPtr, LogwrtResult.Flush))

    2392                 :            :         {

    2406                 :            :          */

    2423                 :            :                          LogwrtResult.Write.xlogid, LogwrtResult.Write.xrecoff,

    2497                 :            : /*
    2498                 :            :  * Create a new XLOG file segment, or open a pre-existing one.
    2499                 :            :  *
    2500                 :            :  * log, seg: identify segment to be created/opened.
    2501                 :            :  *
    2502                 :            :  * *use_existent: if TRUE, OK to use a pre-existing file (else, any
    2503                 :            :  * pre-existing file will be deleted).  On return, TRUE if a pre-existing
    2504                 :            :  * file was used.
    2505                 :            :  *
    2506                 :            :  * use_lock: if TRUE, acquire ControlFileLock while moving file into
    2507                 :            :  * place.  This should be TRUE except during bootstrap log creation.  The
    2508                 :            :  * caller must *not* hold the lock at call.
    2509                 :            :  *
    2510                 :            :  * Returns FD of opened file.
    2511                 :            :  *
    2512                 :            :  * Note: errors here are ERROR not PANIC because we might or might not be
    2513                 :            :  * inside a critical section (eg, during checkpoint there is no reason to
    2514                 :            :  * take down the system on failure).  They will promote to PANIC if we are
    2515                 :            :  * in a critical section.
    2516                 :            :  */
    2517                 :            : static void
    2518                 :            : XLogFileInit(
    2519                 :            :         MirroredFlatFileOpen *mirroredOpen,
    2520                 :            :         uint32 log, uint32 seg,
    2521                 :            :         bool *use_existent, bool use_lock)
    2522                 :            : {
    2523                 :            :         char            simpleFileName[MAXPGPATH];
    2524                 :            :         char            tmpsimple[MAXPGPATH];

    2528                 :            :         uint32          installed_log;
    2529                 :            :         uint32          installed_seg;
    2530                 :            :         int                     max_advance;
    2531                 :            :         int                     nbytes;
    2532                 :       2191 :         char                    *xlogDir = NULL;
    2533                 :            :

    2565                 :            :         /*
    2566                 :            :          * Initialize an empty (all zeroes) segment.  NOTE: it is possible that
    2567                 :            :          * another process is doing the same thing.  If so, we will end up
    2568                 :            :          * pre-creating an extra log segment.  That seems OK, and better than
    2569                 :            :          * holding the lock throughout this lengthy process.
    2570                 :            :          */
    2571                 :         39 :         elog(DEBUG2, "creating and filling new WAL file");
    2572                 :            : 
    2573                 :         39 :         xlogDir = makeRelativeToTxnFilespace(XLOGDIR);
    2574                 :            :

    2580                 :            : 
    2581         [ -  + ]:         39 :         if (snprintf(tmppath, MAXPGPATH, "%s/%s", xlogDir, tmpsimple) > MAXPGPATH)
    2582                 :            :         {
    2583         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2584                 :            :                                 (errmsg("could not generate filename %s/%s", xlogDir, tmpsimple)));
    2585                 :          0 :         }
    2586                 :            : 
    2587                 :         39 :         MirroredFlatFile_Drop(
    2588                 :            :                                                   XLOGDIR,
    2589                 :         39 :                                                   tmpsimple,
    2590                 :            :                                                   /* suppressError */ true,
    2591                 :            :                                                   /*isMirrorRecovery */ false);
    2592                 :            : 
    2593                 :            :         /* do not use XLOG_SYNC_BIT here --- want to fsync only at end of fill */
    2594                 :         39 :         MirroredFlatFile_Open(
    2595                 :            :                                                 &tmpMirroredOpen,
    2596                 :            :                                                 XLOGDIR,
    2597                 :         39 :                                                 tmpsimple,
    2598                 :            :                                                 O_RDWR | O_CREAT | O_EXCL | PG_BINARY,
    2599                 :            :                                             S_IRUSR | S_IWUSR,
    2600                 :            :                                             /* suppressError */ false,
    2601                 :            :                                                 /* atomic operation */ false,
    2602                 :            :                                                 /*isMirrorRecovery */ false);
    2603                 :            : 
    2604                 :            :         /*
    2605                 :            :          * Zero-fill the file.  We have to do this the hard way to ensure that all
    2606                 :            :          * the file space has really been allocated --- on platforms that allow
    2607                 :            :          * "holes" in files, just seeking to the end doesn't allocate intermediate
    2608                 :            :          * space.  This way, we know that we have all the space and (after the
    2609                 :            :          * fsync below) that all the indirect blocks are down on disk.  Therefore,
    2610                 :            :          * fdatasync(2) or O_DSYNC will be sufficient to sync future writes to the
    2611                 :            :          * log file.
    2612                 :            :          *
    2613                 :            :          * Note: palloc zbuffer, instead of just using a local char array, to
    2614                 :            :          * ensure it is reasonably well-aligned; this may save a few cycles
    2615                 :            :          * transferring data to the kernel.
    2616                 :            :          */
    2617                 :         39 :         zbuffer = (char *) palloc0(XLOG_BLCKSZ);
    2618         [ +  + ]:      79911 :         for (nbytes = 0; nbytes < XLogSegSize; nbytes += XLOG_BLCKSZ)
    2619                 :            :         {
    2620                 :      79872 :                 errno = 0;
    2621         [ -  + ]:      79872 :                 if (MirroredFlatFile_Append(
    2622                 :            :                                                         &tmpMirroredOpen,
    2623                 :      79872 :                                                         zbuffer,
    2624                 :            :                                                         XLOG_BLCKSZ,
    2625                 :            :                                                         /* suppressError */ true))
    2626                 :            :                 {
    2627                 :          0 :                         int                     save_errno = errno;
    2628                 :            : 
    2629                 :            :                         /*
    2630                 :            :                          * If we fail to make the file, delete it to release disk space
    2631                 :            :                          */
    2632                 :          0 :                         MirroredFlatFile_Drop(
    2633                 :            :                                                         XLOGDIR,
    2634                 :          0 :                                                         tmpsimple,
    2635                 :            :                                                         /* suppressError */ false,
    2636                 :            :                                                         /*isMirrorRecovery */ false);
    2637                 :            : 
    2638                 :            :                         /* if write didn't set errno, assume problem is no disk space */
    2639         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         errno = save_errno ? save_errno : ENOSPC;
    2640                 :            : 
    2641         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    2642                 :            :                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),
    2643                 :            :                                          errmsg("could not write to file \"%s\": %m", tmppath)));
    2644                 :          0 :                 }
    2645                 :      79872 :         }
    2646                 :         39 :         pfree(zbuffer);
    2647                 :            : 
    2648                 :         39 :         MirroredFlatFile_Flush(
    2649                 :            :                                 &tmpMirroredOpen,
    2650                 :            :                                 /* suppressError */ false);
    2651                 :            :

    2653                 :            : 
    2654                 :            :         /*
    2655                 :            :          * Now move the segment into place with its final name.
    2656                 :            :          *

    2658                 :            :          * pre-existing file.  Otherwise, cope with possibility that someone else

    2660                 :            :          * pre-create a future log segment.
    2661                 :            :          */

    2678                 :            :                                                 /*isMirrorRecovery */ false);
    2679                 :          0 :         }

    2690                 :            :                                             S_IRUSR | S_IWUSR,

    2692                 :            :                                                 /* atomic operation */ false,

    2700                 :            : /*
    2701                 :            :  * Create a new XLOG file segment by copying a pre-existing one.
    2702                 :            :  *
    2703                 :            :  * log, seg: identify segment to be created.
    2704                 :            :  *
    2705                 :            :  * srcTLI, srclog, srcseg: identify segment to be copied (could be from
    2706                 :            :  *              a different timeline)
    2707                 :            :  *
    2708                 :            :  * Currently this is only used during recovery, and so there are no locking
    2709                 :            :  * considerations.      But we should be just as tense as XLogFileInit to avoid
    2710                 :            :  * emplacing a bogus file.
    2711                 :            :  */
    2712                 :            : static void
    2713                 :            : XLogFileCopy(uint32 log, uint32 seg,
    2714                 :            :                          TimeLineID srcTLI, uint32 srclog, uint32 srcseg)
    2715                 :            : {
    2716                 :            :         char            path[MAXPGPATH];
    2717                 :            :         char            tmppath[MAXPGPATH];
    2718                 :            :         char            buffer[XLOG_BLCKSZ];
    2719                 :            :         int                     srcfd;
    2720                 :            :         int                     fd;
    2721                 :            :         int                     nbytes;
    2722                 :          0 :         char            *xlogDir = NULL;
    2723                 :            : 
    2724                 :            :         /*
    2725                 :            :          * Open the source file
    2726                 :            :          */
    2727                 :          0 :         XLogFilePath(path, srcTLI, srclog, srcseg);
    2728                 :          0 :         srcfd = BasicOpenFile(path, O_RDONLY | PG_BINARY, 0);
    2729         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (srcfd < 0)
    2730         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2731                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    2732                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not open file \"%s\": %m", path)));
    2733                 :            : 
    2734                 :            :         /*
    2735                 :            :          * Copy into a temp file name.
    2736                 :            :          */
    2737                 :          0 :         xlogDir = makeRelativeToTxnFilespace(XLOGDIR);
    2738 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (snprintf(tmppath, MAXPGPATH, "%s/xlogtemp.%d",
    2739                 :          0 :                                  xlogDir, (int) getpid()) > MAXPGPATH)
    2740         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2741                 :            :                                 (errmsg("could not generate filename %s/xlogtemp.%d",
    2742                 :            :                                                 xlogDir, (int) getpid())));
    2743                 :          0 :         pfree(xlogDir); 
    2744                 :          0 :         unlink(tmppath);
    2745                 :            : 
    2746                 :          0 :         elog((Debug_print_qd_mirroring ? LOG : DEBUG5), "Master Mirroring: copying xlog file '%s' to '%s'",
    2747                 :            :                  path, tmppath);
    2748                 :            : 
    2749                 :            :         /* do not use XLOG_SYNC_BIT here --- want to fsync only at end of fill */
    2750                 :          0 :         fd = BasicOpenFile(tmppath, O_RDWR | O_CREAT | O_EXCL | PG_BINARY,
    2751                 :            :                                            S_IRUSR | S_IWUSR);
    2752         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (fd < 0)
    2753         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,

    2757                 :            :         /*
    2758                 :            :          * Do the data copying.
    2759                 :            :          */

    2761                 :            :         {

    2784                 :            :

    2790                 :            :

    2808                 :          0 : }
    2809                 :            : 
    2810                 :            : /*
    2811                 :            :  * Install a new XLOG segment file as a current or future log segment.
    2812                 :            :  *
    2813                 :            :  * This is used both to install a newly-created segment (which has a temp
    2814                 :            :  * filename while it's being created) and to recycle an old segment.
    2815                 :            :  *
    2816                 :            :  * *log, *seg: identify segment to install as (or first possible target).
    2817                 :            :  * When find_free is TRUE, these are modified on return to indicate the
    2818                 :            :  * actual installation location or last segment searched.
    2819                 :            :  *
    2820                 :            :  * tmppath: initial name of file to install.  It will be renamed into place.
    2821                 :            :  *
    2822                 :            :  * find_free: if TRUE, install the new segment at the first empty log/seg
    2823                 :            :  * number at or after the passed numbers.  If FALSE, install the new segment
    2824                 :            :  * exactly where specified, deleting any existing segment file there.
    2825                 :            :  *
    2826                 :            :  * *max_advance: maximum number of log/seg slots to advance past the starting
    2827                 :            :  * point.  Fail if no free slot is found in this range.  On return, reduced
    2828                 :            :  * by the number of slots skipped over.  (Irrelevant, and may be NULL,
    2829                 :            :  * when find_free is FALSE.)
    2830                 :            :  *
    2831                 :            :  * use_lock: if TRUE, acquire ControlFileLock while moving file into
    2832                 :            :  * place.  This should be TRUE except during bootstrap log creation.  The
    2833                 :            :  * caller must *not* hold the lock at call.
    2834                 :            :  *
    2835                 :            :  * Returns TRUE if the file was installed successfully.  FALSE indicates that
    2836                 :            :  * max_advance limit was exceeded, or an error occurred while renaming the
    2837                 :            :  * file into place.
    2838                 :            :  */
    2839                 :            : static bool
    2840                 :            : InstallXLogFileSegment(uint32 *log, uint32 *seg, char *tmppath,
    2841                 :            :                                            bool find_free, int *max_advance,
    2842                 :            :                                            bool use_lock, char* tmpsimpleFileName)
    2843                 :            : {
    2844                 :            :         char            path[MAXPGPATH];
    2845                 :            :         char            simpleFileName[MAXPGPATH];
    2846                 :            :         struct stat stat_buf;
    2847                 :         46 :         int retval = 0;
    2848                 :            : 
    2849                 :         46 :         errno = 0;
    2850                 :            : 
    2851                 :         46 :         XLogFileName(simpleFileName, ThisTimeLineID, *log, *seg);
    2852                 :            : 
    2853                 :         46 :         XLogFilePath(path, ThisTimeLineID, *log, *seg);
    2854                 :            : 
    2855                 :            :         /*
    2856                 :            :          * We want to be sure that only one process does this at a time.
    2857                 :            :          */
    2858         [ +  - ]:         46 :         if (use_lock)
    2859                 :         46 :                 LWLockAcquire(ControlFileLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
    2860                 :            : 
    2861         [ -  + ]:         46 :         if (!find_free)
    2862                 :            :         {
    2863                 :            :                 /* Force installation: get rid of any pre-existing segment file */
    2864         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (tmpsimpleFileName) {
    2865                 :            : 
    2866                 :          0 :                         MirroredFlatFile_Drop(
    2867                 :            :                                                                   XLOGDIR,
    2868                 :          0 :                                                                   simpleFileName,
    2869                 :            :                                                                   /* suppressError */ true,
    2870                 :            :                                                                   /*isMirrorRecovery */ false);
    2871                 :          0 :                 } else {
    2872                 :          0 :                         unlink(path);
    2873                 :            :                 }
    2874                 :          0 :         }
    2875                 :            :         else
    2876                 :            :         {
    2877                 :            :                 /* Find a free slot to put it in */
    2878         [ +  + ]:         55 :                 while (stat(path, &stat_buf) == 0)
    2879                 :            :                 {
    2880         [ -  + ]:          9 :                         if (*max_advance <= 0)
    2881                 :            :                         {
    2882                 :            :                                 /* Failed to find a free slot within specified range */
    2883         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (use_lock)
    2884                 :          0 :                                         LWLockRelease(ControlFileLock);
    2885                 :          0 :                                 return false;
    2886                 :            :                         }
    2887         [ -  + ]:          9 :                         NextLogSeg(*log, *seg);
    2888                 :          9 :                         (*max_advance)--;
    2889                 :            : 
    2890                 :          9 :                         XLogFileName(simpleFileName, ThisTimeLineID, *log, *seg);
    2891                 :          9 :                         XLogFilePath(path, ThisTimeLineID, *log, *seg);
    2892                 :            :                 }
    2893                 :            :         }
    2894                 :            : 
    2895                 :            :         /*
    2896                 :            :          * Prefer link() to rename() here just to be really sure that we don't
    2897                 :            :          * overwrite an existing logfile.  However, there shouldn't be one, so
    2898                 :            :          * rename() is an acceptable substitute except for the truly paranoid.
    2899                 :            :          */
    2900                 :            : #if HAVE_WORKING_LINK
    2901                 :            : 
    2902         [ +  - ]:         46 :         if (tmpsimpleFileName) {
    2903                 :         46 :                 retval = MirroredFlatFile_Rename(
    2904                 :            :                                                                                  XLOGDIR,
    2905                 :         46 :                                                                                  /* old name */ tmpsimpleFileName,
    2906                 :         46 :                                                                                  /* new name */ simpleFileName,
    2907                 :            :                                                                                  /* can exist? */ false,
    2908                 :            :                                                                                  /* isMirrorRecovery */ false);
    2909                 :         46 :         } else {
    2910                 :          0 :                 retval = link(tmppath, path);
    2911                 :            :         }
    2912                 :            : 
    2913         [ -  + ]:         46 :         if (retval < 0)
    2914                 :            :         {
    2915         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (use_lock)
    2916                 :          0 :                         LWLockRelease(ControlFileLock);
    2917         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(LOG,
    2918                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    2919                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not link file \"%s\" to \"%s\" (initialization of log file %u, segment %u): %m",
    2920                 :            :                                                 tmppath, path, *log, *seg)));
    2921                 :          0 :                 return false;
    2922                 :            :         }
    2923                 :            : 
    2924         [ +  - ]:         46 :         if (tmpsimpleFileName) {
    2925                 :            : 
    2926                 :         46 :                 MirroredFlatFile_Drop(
    2927                 :            :                                                   XLOGDIR,
    2928                 :         46 :                                                   tmpsimpleFileName,
    2929                 :            :                                                   /* suppressError */ true,
    2930                 :            :                                                   /*isMirrorRecovery */ false);
    2931                 :         46 :         } else {
    2932                 :          0 :                 unlink(tmppath);
    2933                 :            :         }
    2934                 :            : 
    2935                 :            : #else
    2936                 :            :         if (tmpsimpleFileName) {
    2937                 :            :                 retval = MirroredFlatFile_Rename(
    2938                 :            :                                                   XLOGDIR,
    2939                 :            :                                                   /* old name */ tmpsimpleFileName,
    2940                 :            :                                                   /* new name */ simpleFileName,
    2941                 :            :                                                   /* can exist */ false,
    2942                 :            :                                                         /* isMirrorRecovery */ false);
    2943                 :            :         } else {
    2944                 :            :                 retval = rename(tmppath, path);
    2945                 :            :         }
    2946                 :            : 
    2947                 :            :         if (retval < 0)
    2948                 :            :         {
    2949                 :            :                 if (use_lock)
    2950                 :            :                         LWLockRelease(ControlFileLock);
    2951                 :            :                 ereport(LOG,
    2952                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    2953                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not rename file \"%s\" to \"%s\" (initialization of log file %u, segment %u): %m",
    2954                 :            :                                                 tmppath, path, *log, *seg)));
    2955                 :            :                 return false;
    2956                 :            :         }
    2957                 :            : #endif
    2958                 :            : 
    2959         [ +  - ]:         46 :         if (use_lock)
    2960                 :         46 :                 LWLockRelease(ControlFileLock);
    2961                 :            : 
    2962                 :         46 :         return true;
    2963                 :         46 : }
    2964                 :            : 
    2965                 :            : /*
    2966                 :            :  * Open a pre-existing logfile segment for writing.
    2967                 :            :  */
    2968                 :            : static void

    2999                 :            : /*
    3000                 :            :  * Close the current logfile segment for writing.

    3002                 :            : static void
    3003                 :            : XLogFileClose(void)
    3004                 :            : {
    3005 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        447 :         Assert(MirroredFlatFile_IsActive(&mirroredLogFileOpen));
    3006                 :            : 
    3007                 :            :         /*
    3008                 :            :          * posix_fadvise is problematic on many platforms: on older x86 Linux it
    3009                 :            :          * just dumps core, and there are reports of problems on PPC platforms as
    3010                 :            :          * well.  The following is therefore disabled for the time being. We could
    3011                 :            :          * consider some kind of configure test to see if it's safe to use, but
    3012                 :            :          * since we lack hard evidence that there's any useful performance gain to
    3013                 :            :          * be had, spending time on that seems unprofitable for now.
    3014                 :            :          */
    3015                 :            : #ifdef NOT_USED
    3016                 :            : 
    3017                 :            :         /*
    3018                 :            :          * WAL segment files will not be re-read in normal operation, so we advise
    3019                 :            :          * OS to release any cached pages.      But do not do so if WAL archiving is
    3020                 :            :          * active, because archiver process could use the cache to read the WAL
    3021                 :            :          * segment.
    3022                 :            :          *
    3023                 :            :          * While O_DIRECT works for O_SYNC, posix_fadvise() works for fsync() and
    3024                 :            :          * O_SYNC, and some platforms only have posix_fadvise().
    3025                 :            :          */
    3026                 :            : #if defined(HAVE_DECL_POSIX_FADVISE) && defined(POSIX_FADV_DONTNEED)
    3027                 :            :         if (!XLogArchivingActive())
    3028                 :            :                 posix_fadvise(openLogFile, 0, 0, POSIX_FADV_DONTNEED);
    3029                 :            : #endif
    3030                 :            : #endif   /* NOT_USED */
    3031                 :            : 
    3032                 :        447 :         MirroredFlatFile_Close(&mirroredLogFileOpen);
    3033                 :        447 : }

    3035                 :            : /*
    3036                 :            :  * Preallocate log files beyond the specified log endpoint.
    3037                 :            :  *
    3038                 :            :  * XXX this is currently extremely conservative, since it forces only one
    3039                 :            :  * future log segment to exist, and even that only if we are 75% done with
    3040                 :            :  * the current one.  This is only appropriate for very low-WAL-volume systems.
    3041                 :            :  * High-volume systems will be OK once they've built up a sufficient set of
    3042                 :            :  * recycled log segments, but the startup transient is likely to include
    3043                 :            :  * a lot of segment creations by foreground processes, which is not so good.

    3062                 :            :                         &mirroredOpen,

    3160                 :            :                  * complicated.

    3186                 :          7 :                                         InstallXLogFileSegment(&endlogId, &endlogSeg, path,
    3187                 :          7 :                                                                                    true, &max_advance, true, xlde->d_name))
    3188                 :            :                                 {
    3189         [ -  + ]:          7 :                                         ereport(DEBUG2,

    3204                 :            : 
    3205         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         ereport(DEBUG2,

    3221                 :            :                                          * again at the next checkpoint.
    3222                 :            :                                          */
    3223                 :            :                                         snprintf(newpath, MAXPGPATH, "%s.deleted", path);

    3225                 :            :                                         {
    3226                 :            :                                                 ereport(LOG,
    3227                 :            :                                                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    3228                 :            :                                                                  errmsg("could not rename old transaction log file \"%s\": %m",
    3229                 :            :                                                                                 path)));
    3230                 :            :                                                 continue;
    3231                 :            :                                         }

    3238                 :            : #else
    3239                 :          0 :                                         rc = MirroredFlatFile_Drop(
    3240                 :            :                                                                                   XLOGDIR,
    3241                 :          0 :                                                                                   xlde->d_name,
    3242                 :            :                                                                                   /* suppressError */ true,
    3243                 :            :                                                                                   /*isMirrorRecovery */ false);
    3244                 :            : #endif
    3245                 :            : 
    3246         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (rc != 0)
    3247                 :            :                                         {
    3248         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 ereport(LOG,
    3249                 :            :                                                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    3250                 :            :                                                                  errmsg("could not remove old transaction log file \"%s\": %m",
    3251                 :            :                                                                                 path)));
    3252                 :          0 :                                                 continue;
    3253                 :            :                                         }
    3254                 :            : 
    3255                 :          0 :                                         CheckpointStats.ckpt_segs_removed++;

    3351                 :            : /*
    3352                 :            :  * Restore the backup blocks present in an XLOG record, if any.
    3353                 :            :  *
    3354                 :            :  * We assume all of the record has been read into memory at *record.
    3355                 :            :  *
    3356                 :            :  * Note: when a backup block is available in XLOG, we restore it
    3357                 :            :  * unconditionally, even if the page in the database appears newer.
    3358                 :            :  * This is to protect ourselves against database pages that were partially
    3359                 :            :  * or incorrectly written during a crash.  We assume that the XLOG data
    3360                 :            :  * must be good because it has passed a CRC check, while the database
    3361                 :            :  * page might not be.  This will force us to replay all subsequent
    3362                 :            :  * modifications of the page that appear in XLOG, rather than possibly
    3363                 :            :  * ignoring them as already applied, but that's not a huge drawback.
    3364                 :            :  */
    3365                 :            : static void
    3366                 :            : RestoreBkpBlocks(XLogRecord *record, XLogRecPtr lsn)
    3367                 :            : {
    3368                 :        414 :         MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_DECLARE;
    3369                 :            : 
    3370                 :            :         Relation        reln;
    3371                 :            :         Buffer          buffer;
    3372                 :            :         Page            page;
    3373                 :            :         BkpBlock        bkpb;
    3374                 :            :         char       *blk;
    3375                 :            :         int                     i;
    3376                 :            : 
    3377                 :        414 :         blk = (char *) XLogRecGetData(record) + record->xl_len;
    3378         [ +  + ]:       1656 :         for (i = 0; i < XLR_MAX_BKP_BLOCKS; i++)
    3379                 :            :         {
    3380         [ +  + ]:       1242 :                 if (!(record->xl_info & XLR_SET_BKP_BLOCK(i)))
    3381                 :        828 :                         continue;
    3382                 :            : 
    3383                 :        414 :                 memcpy(&bkpb, blk, sizeof(BkpBlock));
    3384                 :        414 :                 blk += sizeof(BkpBlock);
    3385                 :            : 
    3386                 :        414 :                 reln = XLogOpenRelation(bkpb.node);
    3387                 :            : 
    3388                 :            :                 // -------- MirroredLock ----------
    3389 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:        414 :                 MIRROREDLOCK_BUFMGR_LOCK;
         [ #  # ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
    3390                 :            : 
    3391                 :        414 :                 buffer = XLogReadBuffer(reln, bkpb.block, true);
    3392 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:        414 :                 Assert(BufferIsValid(buffer));
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
    3393 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:        414 :                 page = (Page) BufferGetPage(buffer);
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
                 [ -  + ]
    3394                 :            : 
    3395         [ -  + ]:        414 :                 if (bkpb.hole_length == 0)
    3396                 :            :                 {
    3397                 :          0 :                         memcpy((char *) page, blk, BLCKSZ);

    3421                 :            : /*
    3422                 :            :  * CRC-check an XLOG record.  We do not believe the contents of an XLOG
    3423                 :            :  * record (other than to the minimal extent of computing the amount of
    3424                 :            :  * data to read in) until we've checked the CRCs.
    3425                 :            :  *
    3426                 :            :  * We assume all of the record has been read into memory at *record.
    3427                 :            :  */
    3428                 :            : static bool
    3429                 :            : RecordIsValid(XLogRecord *record, XLogRecPtr recptr, int emode)
    3430                 :            : {
    3431                 :            :         pg_crc32        crc;
    3432                 :            :         int                     i;
    3433                 :   19132753 :         uint32          len = record->xl_len;
    3434                 :            :         BkpBlock        bkpb;
    3435                 :            :         char       *blk;
    3436                 :            : 
    3437                 :            :         /*
    3438                 :            :          * Calculate the crc using the new fast crc32c algorithm
    3439                 :            :          */
    3440                 :            : 
    3441                 :            :         /* First the rmgr data */
    3442                 :   19132753 :         INIT_CRC32C(crc);
    3443                 :   19132753 :         COMP_CRC32C(crc, XLogRecGetData(record), len);
    3444                 :            : 
    3445                 :            :         /* Add in the backup blocks, if any */
    3446                 :   19132753 :         blk = (char *) XLogRecGetData(record) + len;
    3447         [ +  + ]:   76531012 :         for (i = 0; i < XLR_MAX_BKP_BLOCKS; i++)
    3448                 :            :         {
    3449                 :            :                 uint32          blen;
    3450                 :            : 
    3451         [ +  + ]:   57398259 :                 if (!(record->xl_info & XLR_SET_BKP_BLOCK(i)))
    3452                 :   57397017 :                         continue;
    3453                 :            : 
    3454                 :       1242 :                 memcpy(&bkpb, blk, sizeof(BkpBlock));
    3455         [ -  + ]:       1242 :                 if (bkpb.hole_offset + bkpb.hole_length > BLCKSZ)
    3456                 :            :                 {
    3457         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(emode,
    3458                 :            :                                         (errmsg("incorrect hole size in record at %X/%X",
    3459                 :            :                                                         recptr.xlogid, recptr.xrecoff)));
    3460                 :          0 :                         return false;
    3461                 :            :                 }
    3462                 :       1242 :                 blen = sizeof(BkpBlock) + BLCKSZ - bkpb.hole_length;
    3463                 :       1242 :                 COMP_CRC32C(crc, blk, blen);
    3464                 :       1242 :                 blk += blen;
    3465                 :       1242 :         }
    3466                 :            : 
    3467                 :            :         /* Check that xl_tot_len agrees with our calculation */
    3468         [ -  + ]:   19132753 :         if (blk != (char *) record + record->xl_tot_len)
    3469                 :            :         {
    3470         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(emode,
    3471                 :            :                                 (errmsg("incorrect total length in record at %X/%X",
    3472                 :            :                                                 recptr.xlogid, recptr.xrecoff)));
    3473                 :          0 :                 return false;
    3474                 :            :         }
    3475                 :            : 
    3476                 :            :         /* Finally include the record header */
    3477                 :   19132753 :         COMP_CRC32C(crc, (char *) record + sizeof(pg_crc32),
    3478                 :            :                            SizeOfXLogRecord - sizeof(pg_crc32));
    3479                 :   19132753 :         FIN_CRC32C(crc);
    3480                 :            : 
    3481         [ -  + ]:   19132753 :         if (!EQ_CRC32C(record->xl_crc, crc))
    3482                 :            :         {
    3483         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(emode,
    3484                 :            :                 (errmsg("incorrect resource manager data checksum in record at %X/%X",
    3485                 :            :                                 recptr.xlogid, recptr.xrecoff)));
    3486                 :          0 :                 return false;
    3487                 :            :         }
    3488                 :            : 
    3489                 :   19132753 :         return true;
    3490                 :   19132753 : }

    3643                 :            : /*
    3644                 :            :  * Read the XLOG page containing RecPtr into readBuf (if not read already).

    3648                 :            :  * standby mode.
    3649                 :            :  *
    3650                 :            :  * 'emode' specifies the log level used for reporting "file not found" or
    3651                 :            :  * "end of WAL" situations in standby mode when a trigger file is found.
    3652                 :            :  * If set to WARNING or below, XLogPageRead() returns false in those situations
    3653                 :            :  * on higher log levels the ereport() won't return.
    3654                 :            :  *

    3656                 :            :  * Otherwise it keeps sleeping and retrying indefinitely.

    3746                 :            :

    3751                 :            :                                          * WAL from walreceiver and observe that we had already
    3752                 :            :                                          * processed everything before the most recent "chunk"
    3753                 :            :                                          * that it flushed to disk.  In steady state where we are
    3754                 :            :                                          * keeping up with the incoming data, XLogReceiptTime will
    3755                 :            :                                          * be updated on each cycle.  When we are behind,
    3756                 :            :                                          * XLogReceiptTime will not advance, so the grace time
    3757                 :            :                                          * alloted to conflicting queries will decrease.

    3801                 :          0 :                                                 }

    3822                 :            :

    3879                 :          0 :                                                 }

    4074                 :            :  * (emode must be either PANIC or LOG.)

    4575                 :            :         /*

    4581                 :            :          * across pages; therefore ReadRecord resets lastPageTLI and
    4582                 :            :          * lastSegmentTLI to zero when going to a random page.
    4583                 :            :          *

    4585                 :            :          * In that case we cannot perform the normal TLI check: if there is a
    4586                 :            :          * timeline switch within the segment, the first page has a smaller TLI

    4588                 :            :          * them already. As a weaker test, we still check that it's not smaller
    4589                 :            :          * than the TLI we last saw at the beginning of a segment. Pass

    4608                 :            : /*
    4609                 :            :  * Try to read a timeline's history file.

    4657                 :          0 :                 }

    4663         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (endptr == ptr)

    4698                 :            :  * Probe whether a timeline history file exists for the given timeline ID

    4700                 :            : static bool
    4701                 :            : existsTimeLineHistory(TimeLineID probeTLI)

    4716         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (errno != ENOENT)

    4724                 :            : /*

    4742                 :            :

    4745         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (existsTimeLineHistory(probeTLI))

    4748                 :          0 :                 }

    4750                 :            :                 {
    4751                 :            :                         /* doesn't exist, assume we're done */
    4752                 :          0 :                         break;
    4753                 :            :                 }
    4754                 :          0 :         }
    4755                 :            : 
    4756                 :          0 :         return newestTLI;
    4757                 :            : }

    4759                 :            : /*

    4985                 :            : 
    4986                 :            : /*
    4987                 :            :  * I/O routines for pg_control
    4988                 :            :  *
    4989                 :            :  * *ControlFile is a buffer in shared memory that holds an image of the
    4990                 :            :  * contents of pg_control.      WriteControlFile() initializes pg_control
    4991                 :            :  * given a preloaded buffer, ReadControlFile() loads the buffer from
    4992                 :            :  * the pg_control file (during postmaster or standalone-backend startup),
    4993                 :            :  * and UpdateControlFile() rewrites pg_control after we modify xlog state.
    4994                 :            :  *
    4995                 :            :  * For simplicity, WriteControlFile() initializes the fields of pg_control
    4996                 :            :  * that are related to checking backend/database compatibility, and
    4997                 :            :  * ReadControlFile() verifies they are correct.  We could split out the
    4998                 :            :  * I/O and compatibility-check functions, but there seems no need currently.
    4999                 :            :  */
    5000                 :            : static void
    5001                 :            : WriteControlFile(void)
    5002                 :            : {
    5003                 :            :         MirroredFlatFileOpen    mirroredOpen;
    5004                 :            : 
    5005                 :            :         char            buffer[PG_CONTROL_SIZE];                /* need not be aligned */
    5006                 :            :         char       *localeptr;
    5007                 :            : 
    5008                 :            :         /*
    5009                 :            :          * Initialize version and compatibility-check fields
    5010                 :            :          */
    5011                 :          0 :         ControlFile->pg_control_version = PG_CONTROL_VERSION;
    5012                 :          0 :         ControlFile->catalog_version_no = CATALOG_VERSION_NO;
    5013                 :            : 
    5014                 :          0 :         ControlFile->maxAlign = MAXIMUM_ALIGNOF;
    5015                 :          0 :         ControlFile->floatFormat = FLOATFORMAT_VALUE;
    5016                 :            : 
    5017                 :          0 :         ControlFile->blcksz = BLCKSZ;
    5018                 :          0 :         ControlFile->relseg_size = RELSEG_SIZE;
    5019                 :          0 :         ControlFile->xlog_blcksz = XLOG_BLCKSZ;
    5020                 :          0 :         ControlFile->xlog_seg_size = XLOG_SEG_SIZE;
    5021                 :            : 
    5022                 :          0 :         ControlFile->nameDataLen = NAMEDATALEN;
    5023                 :          0 :         ControlFile->indexMaxKeys = INDEX_MAX_KEYS;
    5024                 :            : 
    5025                 :          0 :         ControlFile->toast_max_chunk_size = TOAST_MAX_CHUNK_SIZE;
    5026                 :            : 
    5027                 :            : #ifdef HAVE_INT64_TIMESTAMP
    5028                 :          0 :         ControlFile->enableIntTimes = TRUE;
    5029                 :            : #else
    5030                 :            :         ControlFile->enableIntTimes = FALSE;
    5031                 :            : #endif
    5032                 :            : 
    5033                 :          0 :         ControlFile->localeBuflen = LOCALE_NAME_BUFLEN;
    5034                 :          0 :         localeptr = setlocale(LC_COLLATE, NULL);
    5035         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!localeptr)
    5036         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(PANIC,
    5037                 :            :                                 (errmsg("invalid LC_COLLATE setting")));
    5038         [ #  # ]:          0 :         StrNCpy(ControlFile->lc_collate, localeptr, LOCALE_NAME_BUFLEN);
    5039                 :          0 :         localeptr = setlocale(LC_CTYPE, NULL);
    5040         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!localeptr)
    5041         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(PANIC,
    5042                 :            :                                 (errmsg("invalid LC_CTYPE setting")));
    5043         [ #  # ]:          0 :         StrNCpy(ControlFile->lc_ctype, localeptr, LOCALE_NAME_BUFLEN);
    5044                 :            : 
    5045                 :            :         /* Contents are protected with a CRC */
    5046                 :          0 :         INIT_CRC32C(ControlFile->crc);
    5047                 :          0 :         COMP_CRC32C(ControlFile->crc,
    5048                 :            :                            (char *) ControlFile,
    5049                 :            :                            offsetof(ControlFileData, crc));
    5050                 :          0 :         FIN_CRC32C(ControlFile->crc);
    5051                 :            : 
    5052                 :            :         /*
    5053                 :            :          * We write out PG_CONTROL_SIZE bytes into pg_control, zero-padding the
    5054                 :            :          * excess over sizeof(ControlFileData).  This reduces the odds of
    5055                 :            :          * premature-EOF errors when reading pg_control.  We'll still fail when we
    5056                 :            :          * check the contents of the file, but hopefully with a more specific
    5057                 :            :          * error than "couldn't read pg_control".
    5058                 :            :          */
    5059                 :            :         if (sizeof(ControlFileData) > PG_CONTROL_SIZE)
    5060                 :            :                 elog(PANIC, "sizeof(ControlFileData) is larger than PG_CONTROL_SIZE; fix either one");
    5061                 :            : 
    5062                 :          0 :         memset(buffer, 0, PG_CONTROL_SIZE);
    5063                 :          0 :         memcpy(buffer, ControlFile, sizeof(ControlFileData));
    5064                 :            : 
    5065                 :          0 :         MirroredFlatFile_Open(

    5290                 :            : static bool
    5291                 :            : XLogGetWriteAndFlushedLoc(XLogRecPtr *writeLoc, XLogRecPtr *flushedLoc)
    5292                 :            : {
    5293                 :            :         /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */
    5294                 :         24 :         volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
    5295                 :            : 
    5296         [ -  + ]:         24 :         SpinLockAcquire(&xlogctl->info_lck);

    5302                 :            : }
    5303                 :            : 
    5304                 :            : /*
    5305                 :            :  * Very specific purpose routine for FileRep that flushes out XLOG records from the
    5306                 :            :  * XLOG memory cache to disk.
    5307                 :            :  */
    5308                 :            : void
    5309                 :            : XLogFileRepFlushCache(
    5310                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      *lastChangeTrackingEndLoc)
    5311                 :            : {
    5312                 :            :         /*
    5313                 :            :          * We hold the ChangeTrackingTransitionLock EXCLUSIVE, thus the lastChangeTrackingEndLoc
    5314                 :            :          * value is the previous location -- the one we want.
    5315                 :            :          *
    5316                 :            :          * Since the lock is acquired after ALL WRITES and FSYNCS in XLogInsert_Internal,
    5317                 :            :          * we know this flush is safe (i.e. will not hang) and will push out all XLOG records we
    5318                 :            :          * want to see in the next call to ChangeTracking_CreateInitialFromPreviousCheckpoint.
    5319                 :            :          */
    5320                 :            : 
    5321                 :          0 :         *lastChangeTrackingEndLoc = XLogCtl->lastChangeTrackingEndLoc;
    5322                 :            : 
    5323         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Debug_persistent_print)
    5324                 :          0 :                 elog(Persistent_DebugPrintLevel(),
    5325                 :            :                          "XLogFileRepFlushCache: Going flush through location %s...",
    5326                 :            :                          XLogLocationToString(lastChangeTrackingEndLoc));
    5327                 :            : 
    5328                 :          0 :         XLogFlush(*lastChangeTrackingEndLoc);
    5329                 :          0 : }
    5330                 :            : 
    5331                 :            : void
    5332                 :            : XLogInChangeTrackingTransition(void)
    5333                 :            : {
    5334                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      lastChangeTrackingEndLoc;
    5335                 :          0 :         XLogRecPtr      recPtrInit = {0, 0};
    5336                 :            : 
    5337         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Debug_persistent_print)
    5338                 :          0 :                 elog(Persistent_DebugPrintLevel(),
    5339                 :            :                          "XLogInChangeTrackingTransition: Acquiring ChangeTrackingTransitionLock...");
    5340                 :            : 
    5341                 :          0 :         LWLockAcquire(ChangeTrackingTransitionLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
    5342                 :            : 
    5343         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Debug_persistent_print)
    5344                 :          0 :                 elog(Persistent_DebugPrintLevel(),
    5345                 :            :                          "XLogInChangeTrackingTransition: SegmentStateInChangeTrackingTransition...");
    5346                 :            : 
    5347                 :          0 :         FileRep_SetSegmentState(SegmentStateInChangeTrackingTransition, FaultTypeNotInitialized);
    5348                 :            : 
    5349                 :          0 :         XLogFileRepFlushCache(&lastChangeTrackingEndLoc);
    5350                 :            : 
    5351         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Debug_persistent_print)
    5352                 :          0 :                 elog(Persistent_DebugPrintLevel(),
    5353                 :            :                          "XLogInChangeTrackingTransition: Calling ChangeTracking_CreateInitialFromPreviousCheckpoint with lastChangeTrackingEndLoc %s",
    5354                 :            :                          XLogLocationToString(&lastChangeTrackingEndLoc));
    5355                 :            : 
    5356                 :            :         /*
    5357                 :            :          * During gpstart the following order is followed for recovery:
    5358                 :            :          *                      a) xlog records from last checkpoint are replayed into change tracking log file
    5359                 :            :          *                      b) xlog is replayed by 3 pass mechanism
    5360                 :            :          * In that case XLogCtl->lastChangeTrackingEndLoc will be still set to {0,0} and
    5361                 :            :          * in order to insert all xlog records from last checkpoint into change tracking log file
    5362                 :            :          * ChangeTracking_CreateInitialFromPreviousCheckpoint(NULL); has to be called.
    5363                 :            :          */
    5364 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (XLByteEQ(lastChangeTrackingEndLoc, recPtrInit))
    5365                 :            :         {
    5366                 :          0 :                 ChangeTracking_CreateInitialFromPreviousCheckpoint(NULL);
    5367                 :          0 :         }
    5368                 :            :         else
    5369                 :            :         {
    5370                 :          0 :                 ChangeTracking_CreateInitialFromPreviousCheckpoint(&lastChangeTrackingEndLoc);
    5371                 :            :         }
    5372                 :            : 
    5373                 :          0 :         LWLockRelease(ChangeTrackingTransitionLock);
    5374                 :            : 
    5375         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Debug_persistent_print)
    5376                 :          0 :                 elog(Persistent_DebugPrintLevel(),
    5377                 :            :                          "XLogInChangeTrackingTransition: Released ChangeTrackingTransitionLock");
    5378                 :            : 
    5379                 :          0 : }

    5440                 :            : /*
    5441                 :            :  * Initialization of shared memory for XLOG
    5442                 :            :  */
    5443                 :            : Size
    5444                 :            : XLOGShmemSize(void)
    5445                 :            : {
    5446                 :            :         Size            size;
    5447                 :            : 
    5448                 :            :         /* XLogCtl */
    5449                 :      46770 :         size = sizeof(XLogCtlData);
    5450                 :            :         /* xlblocks array */
    5451                 :      46770 :         size = add_size(size, mul_size(sizeof(XLogRecPtr), XLOGbuffers));
    5452                 :            :         /* extra alignment padding for XLOG I/O buffers */
    5453                 :      46770 :         size = add_size(size, ALIGNOF_XLOG_BUFFER);
    5454                 :            :         /* and the buffers themselves */
    5455                 :      46770 :         size = add_size(size, mul_size(XLOG_BLCKSZ, XLOGbuffers));
    5456                 :            : 
    5457                 :            :         /*
    5458                 :            :          * Note: we don't count ControlFileData, it comes out of the "slop factor"
    5459                 :            :          * added by CreateSharedMemoryAndSemaphores.  This lets us use this
    5460                 :            :          * routine again below to compute the actual allocation size.
    5461                 :            :          */
    5462                 :            : 
    5463                 :            :         /*

    5540                 :            : /**

    5557                 :            : 
    5558                 :            : /*
    5559                 :            :  * This func must be called ONCE on system install.  It creates pg_control
    5560                 :            :  * and the initial XLOG segment.
    5561                 :            :  */
    5562                 :            : void
    5563                 :            : BootStrapXLOG(void)
    5564                 :            : {
    5565                 :            :         CheckPoint      checkPoint;
    5566                 :            :         char       *buffer;
    5567                 :            :         XLogPageHeader page;
    5568                 :            :         XLogLongPageHeader longpage;
    5569                 :            :         XLogRecord *record;
    5570                 :            :         bool            use_existent;
    5571                 :            :         uint64          sysidentifier;
    5572                 :            :         struct timeval tv;
    5573                 :            :         pg_crc32        crc;
    5574                 :            : 
    5575                 :            :         /*
    5576                 :            :          * Select a hopefully-unique system identifier code for this installation.
    5577                 :            :          * We use the result of gettimeofday(), including the fractional seconds
    5578                 :            :          * field, as being about as unique as we can easily get.  (Think not to
    5579                 :            :          * use random(), since it hasn't been seeded and there's no portable way
    5580                 :            :          * to seed it other than the system clock value...)  The upper half of the
    5581                 :            :          * uint64 value is just the tv_sec part, while the lower half is the XOR
    5582                 :            :          * of tv_sec and tv_usec.  This is to ensure that we don't lose uniqueness
    5583                 :            :          * unnecessarily if "uint64" is really only 32 bits wide.  A person
    5584                 :            :          * knowing this encoding can determine the initialization time of the
    5585                 :            :          * installation, which could perhaps be useful sometimes.
    5586                 :            :          */
    5587                 :          0 :         gettimeofday(&tv, NULL);
    5588                 :          0 :         sysidentifier = ((uint64) tv.tv_sec) << 32;
    5589                 :          0 :         sysidentifier |= (uint32) (tv.tv_sec | tv.tv_usec);
    5590                 :            : 
    5591                 :            :         /* First timeline ID is always 1 */
    5592                 :          0 :         ThisTimeLineID = 1;
    5593                 :            : 
    5594                 :            :         /* page buffer must be aligned suitably for O_DIRECT */
    5595                 :          0 :         buffer = (char *) palloc(XLOG_BLCKSZ + ALIGNOF_XLOG_BUFFER);
    5596                 :          0 :         page = (XLogPageHeader) TYPEALIGN(ALIGNOF_XLOG_BUFFER, buffer);
    5597                 :          0 :         memset(page, 0, XLOG_BLCKSZ);
    5598                 :            : 
    5599                 :            :         /*
    5600                 :            :          * Set up information for the initial checkpoint record
    5601                 :            :          *
    5602                 :            :          * The initial checkpoint record is written to the beginning of the WAL
    5603                 :            :          * segment with logid=0 logseg=1. The very first WAL segment, 0/0, is not
    5604                 :            :          * used, so that we can use 0/0 to mean "before any valid WAL segment".
    5605                 :            :          */
    5606                 :          0 :         checkPoint.redo.xlogid = 0;
    5607                 :          0 :         checkPoint.redo.xrecoff = XLogSegSize + SizeOfXLogLongPHD;
    5608                 :          0 :         checkPoint.ThisTimeLineID = ThisTimeLineID;
    5609                 :          0 :         checkPoint.nextXidEpoch = 0;
    5610                 :          0 :         checkPoint.nextXid = FirstNormalTransactionId;
    5611                 :          0 :         checkPoint.nextOid = FirstBootstrapObjectId;
    5612                 :          0 :         checkPoint.nextRelfilenode = FirstNormalObjectId;
    5613                 :          0 :         checkPoint.nextMulti = FirstMultiXactId;
    5614                 :          0 :         checkPoint.nextMultiOffset = 0;
    5615                 :          0 :         checkPoint.time = time(NULL);
    5616                 :            : 
    5617                 :          0 :         ShmemVariableCache->nextXid = checkPoint.nextXid;
    5618                 :          0 :         ShmemVariableCache->nextOid = checkPoint.nextOid;
    5619                 :          0 :         ShmemVariableCache->oidCount = 0;
    5620                 :          0 :         ShmemVariableCache->nextRelfilenode = checkPoint.nextRelfilenode;
    5621                 :          0 :         ShmemVariableCache->relfilenodeCount = 0;
    5622                 :          0 :         MultiXactSetNextMXact(checkPoint.nextMulti, checkPoint.nextMultiOffset);
    5623                 :            : 
    5624                 :            :         /* Set up the XLOG page header */
    5625                 :          0 :         page->xlp_magic = XLOG_PAGE_MAGIC;
    5626                 :          0 :         page->xlp_info = XLP_LONG_HEADER;
    5627                 :          0 :         page->xlp_tli = ThisTimeLineID;
    5628                 :          0 :         page->xlp_pageaddr.xlogid = 0;
    5629                 :          0 :         page->xlp_pageaddr.xrecoff = XLogSegSize;
    5630                 :          0 :         longpage = (XLogLongPageHeader) page;
    5631                 :          0 :         longpage->xlp_sysid = sysidentifier;
    5632                 :          0 :         longpage->xlp_seg_size = XLogSegSize;
    5633                 :          0 :         longpage->xlp_xlog_blcksz = XLOG_BLCKSZ;
    5634                 :            : 
    5635                 :            :         /* Insert the initial checkpoint record */
    5636                 :          0 :         record = (XLogRecord *) ((char *) page + SizeOfXLogLongPHD);
    5637                 :          0 :         record->xl_prev.xlogid = 0;
    5638                 :          0 :         record->xl_prev.xrecoff = 0;
    5639                 :          0 :         record->xl_xid = InvalidTransactionId;
    5640                 :          0 :         record->xl_tot_len = SizeOfXLogRecord + sizeof(checkPoint);
    5641                 :          0 :         record->xl_len = sizeof(checkPoint);
    5642                 :          0 :         record->xl_info = XLOG_CHECKPOINT_SHUTDOWN;
    5643                 :          0 :         record->xl_rmid = RM_XLOG_ID;
    5644                 :          0 :         memcpy(XLogRecGetData(record), &checkPoint, sizeof(checkPoint));
    5645                 :            : 
    5646                 :          0 :         INIT_CRC32C(crc);
    5647                 :          0 :         COMP_CRC32C(crc, &checkPoint, sizeof(checkPoint));
    5648                 :          0 :         COMP_CRC32C(crc, (char *) record + sizeof(pg_crc32),
    5649                 :            :                            SizeOfXLogRecord - sizeof(pg_crc32));
    5650                 :          0 :         FIN_CRC32C(crc);
    5651                 :            : 
    5652                 :          0 :         record->xl_crc = crc;
    5653                 :            : 
    5654                 :            :         /* Create first XLOG segment file */

    5656                 :          0 :         XLogFileInit(
    5657                 :            :                 &mirroredLogFileOpen,
    5658                 :            :                 0, 1, &use_existent, false);
    5659                 :            : 
    5660                 :            :         /* Write the first page with the initial record */
    5661                 :          0 :         errno = 0;

    5663                 :            :                         &mirroredLogFileOpen,
    5664                 :          0 :                         page,
    5665                 :            :                         XLOG_BLCKSZ,
    5666                 :            :                         /* suppressError */ true))
    5667                 :            :         {
    5668         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(PANIC,
    5669                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),

    5671                 :          0 :         }
    5672                 :            : 
    5673         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (MirroredFlatFile_Flush(

    5675                 :            :                         /* suppressError */ true))

    5677                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),

    5679                 :            : 
    5680                 :          0 :         MirroredFlatFile_Close(
    5681                 :            :                         &mirroredLogFileOpen);

    5683                 :            :         /* Now create pg_control */

    5685                 :          0 :         memset(ControlFile, 0, sizeof(ControlFileData));
