    8145                 :            : /*
    8146                 :            :  * Is the system still in recovery?
    8147                 :            :  *
    8148                 :            :  * Unlike testing InRecovery, this works in any process that's connected to
    8149                 :            :  * shared memory.
    8150                 :            :  *
    8151                 :            :  * As a side-effect, we initialize the local TimeLineID and RedoRecPtr
    8152                 :            :  * variables the first time we see that recovery is finished.
    8153                 :            :  */
    8154                 :            : bool
    8155                 :            : RecoveryInProgress(void)
    8156                 :            : {
    8157                 :            :         /*
    8158                 :            :          * We check shared state each time only until we leave recovery mode. We
    8159                 :            :          * can't re-enter recovery, so there's no need to keep checking after the
    8160                 :            :          * shared variable has once been seen false.
    8161                 :            :          */
    8162         [ -  + ]:      17689 :         if (!LocalRecoveryInProgress)
    8163                 :          0 :                 return false;
    8164                 :            :         else
    8165                 :            :         {
    8166                 :            :                 /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */
    8167                 :      17689 :                 volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
    8168                 :            : 
    8169                 :            :                 /* spinlock is essential on machines with weak memory ordering! */
    8170         [ +  + ]:      17689 :                 SpinLockAcquire(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8171                 :      17689 :                 LocalRecoveryInProgress = xlogctl->SharedRecoveryInProgress;
    8172                 :      17689 :                 SpinLockRelease(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8173                 :            : 
    8174                 :            :                 /*
    8175                 :            :                  * Initialize TimeLineID and RedoRecPtr when we discover that recovery
    8176                 :            :                  * is finished. InitPostgres() relies upon this behaviour to ensure
    8177                 :            :                  * that InitXLOGAccess() is called at backend startup.  (If you change
    8178                 :            :                  * this, see also LocalSetXLogInsertAllowed.)
    8179                 :            :                  */
    8180         [ +  - ]:      17689 :                 if (!LocalRecoveryInProgress)
    8181                 :      17689 :                         InitXLOGAccess();
    8182                 :            : 
    8183                 :      17689 :                 return LocalRecoveryInProgress;
    8184                 :            :         }
    8185                 :      17689 : }

    8340                 :            : static void
    8341                 :            : UnpackCheckPointRecord(

    8404                 :            : /*
    8405                 :            :  * This must be called during startup of a backend process, except that
    8406                 :            :  * it need not be called in a standalone backend (which does StartupXLOG
    8407                 :            :  * instead).  We need to initialize the local copies of ThisTimeLineID and
    8408                 :            :  * RedoRecPtr.
    8409                 :            :  *
    8410                 :            :  * Note: before Postgres 8.0, we went to some effort to keep the postmaster
    8411                 :            :  * process's copies of ThisTimeLineID and RedoRecPtr valid too.  This was
    8412                 :            :  * unnecessary however, since the postmaster itself never touches XLOG anyway.
    8413                 :            :  */
    8414                 :            : void
    8415                 :            : InitXLOGAccess(void)
    8416                 :            : {
    8417                 :            :         /* ThisTimeLineID doesn't change so we need no lock to copy it */
    8418                 :      35429 :         ThisTimeLineID = XLogCtl->ThisTimeLineID;
    8419                 :            :         /* Use GetRedoRecPtr to copy the RedoRecPtr safely */
    8420                 :      35429 :         (void) GetRedoRecPtr();
    8421                 :      35429 : }
    8422                 :            : 
    8423                 :            : /*
    8424                 :            :  * Once spawned, a backend may update its local RedoRecPtr from
    8425                 :            :  * XLogCtl->Insert.RedoRecPtr; it must hold the insert lock or info_lck
    8426                 :            :  * to do so.  This is done in XLogInsert() or GetRedoRecPtr().
    8427                 :            :  */
    8428                 :            : XLogRecPtr
    8429                 :            : GetRedoRecPtr(void)
    8430                 :            : {
    8431                 :            :         /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */
    8432                 :      35452 :         volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
    8433                 :            : 
    8434         [ +  + ]:      35452 :         SpinLockAcquire(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8435 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:      35452 :         Assert(XLByteLE(RedoRecPtr, xlogctl->Insert.RedoRecPtr));
         [ +  - ][ -  + ]
    8436                 :      35452 :         RedoRecPtr = xlogctl->Insert.RedoRecPtr;
    8437                 :      35452 :         SpinLockRelease(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8438                 :            : 
    8439                 :      35452 :         return RedoRecPtr;
    8440                 :            : }
    8441                 :            : 
    8442                 :            : /*
    8443                 :            :  * GetInsertRecPtr -- Returns the current insert position.
    8444                 :            :  *
    8445                 :            :  * NOTE: The value *actually* returned is the position of the last full
    8446                 :            :  * xlog page. It lags behind the real insert position by at most 1 page.
    8447                 :            :  * For that, we don't need to acquire WALInsertLock which can be quite
    8448                 :            :  * heavily contended, and an approximation is enough for the current
    8449                 :            :  * usage of this function.
    8450                 :            :  */
    8451                 :            : XLogRecPtr
    8452                 :            : GetInsertRecPtr(void)
    8453                 :            : {
    8454                 :            :         /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */
    8455                 :          0 :         volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
    8456                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      recptr;
    8457                 :            : 
    8458         [ #  # ]:          0 :         SpinLockAcquire(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8459                 :          0 :         recptr = xlogctl->LogwrtRqst.Write;
    8460                 :          0 :         SpinLockRelease(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8461                 :            : 
    8462                 :          0 :         return recptr;
    8463                 :            : }
    8464                 :            : 
    8465                 :            : /*
    8466                 :            :  * GetFlushRecPtr -- Returns the current flush position, ie, the last WAL
    8467                 :            :  * position known to be fsync'd to disk.
    8468                 :            :  */
    8469                 :            : XLogRecPtr
    8470                 :            : GetFlushRecPtr(void)
    8471                 :            : {
    8472                 :            :         /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */
    8473                 :          0 :         volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
    8474                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      recptr;
    8475                 :            : 
    8476         [ #  # ]:          0 :         SpinLockAcquire(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8477                 :          0 :         recptr = xlogctl->LogwrtResult.Flush;
    8478                 :          0 :         SpinLockRelease(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8479                 :            : 
    8480                 :          0 :         return recptr;
    8481                 :            : }

    8483                 :            : /*
    8484                 :            :  * Get the time of the last xlog segment switch
    8485                 :            :  */
    8486                 :            : time_t
    8487                 :            : GetLastSegSwitchTime(void)
    8488                 :            : {
    8489                 :            :         time_t          result;
    8490                 :            : 
    8491                 :            :         /* Need WALWriteLock, but shared lock is sufficient */
    8492                 :          0 :         LWLockAcquire(WALWriteLock, LW_SHARED);
    8493                 :          0 :         result = XLogCtl->Write.lastSegSwitchTime;
    8494                 :          0 :         LWLockRelease(WALWriteLock);
    8495                 :            : 
    8496                 :          0 :         return result;

    8499                 :            : /*
    8500                 :            :  * GetNextXidAndEpoch - get the current nextXid value and associated epoch
    8501                 :            :  *
    8502                 :            :  * This is exported for use by code that would like to have 64-bit XIDs.
    8503                 :            :  * We don't really support such things, but all XIDs within the system
    8504                 :            :  * can be presumed "close to" the result, and thus the epoch associated
    8505                 :            :  * with them can be determined.
    8506                 :            :  */
    8507                 :            : void
    8508                 :            : GetNextXidAndEpoch(TransactionId *xid, uint32 *epoch)
    8509                 :            : {
    8510                 :            :         uint32          ckptXidEpoch;
    8511                 :            :         TransactionId ckptXid;
    8512                 :            :         TransactionId nextXid;
    8513                 :            : 
    8514                 :            :         /* Must read checkpoint info first, else have race condition */
    8515                 :            :         {
    8516                 :            :                 /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */
    8517                 :          4 :                 volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
    8518                 :            : 
    8519         [ -  + ]:          4 :                 SpinLockAcquire(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8520                 :          4 :                 ckptXidEpoch = xlogctl->ckptXidEpoch;
    8521                 :          4 :                 ckptXid = xlogctl->ckptXid;
    8522                 :          4 :                 SpinLockRelease(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8523                 :            :         }
    8524                 :            : 
    8525                 :            :         /* Now fetch current nextXid */
    8526                 :          4 :         nextXid = ReadNewTransactionId();
    8527                 :            : 
    8528                 :            :         /*
    8529                 :            :          * nextXid is certainly logically later than ckptXid.  So if it's
    8530                 :            :          * numerically less, it must have wrapped into the next epoch.
    8531                 :            :          */
    8532         [ -  + ]:          4 :         if (nextXid < ckptXid)
    8533                 :          0 :                 ckptXidEpoch++;
    8534                 :            : 
    8535                 :          4 :         *xid = nextXid;
    8536                 :          4 :         *epoch = ckptXidEpoch;
    8537                 :          4 : }
    8538                 :            : 
    8539                 :            : /*
    8540                 :            :  * This must be called ONCE during postmaster or standalone-backend shutdown
    8541                 :            :  */
    8542                 :            : void
    8543                 :            : ShutdownXLOG(int code __attribute__((unused)) , Datum arg __attribute__((unused)) )
    8544                 :            : {
    8545         [ +  - ]:          2 :         ereport(LOG,
    8546                 :            :                         (errmsg("shutting down")));
    8547                 :            : 
    8548                 :            :         /*
    8549                 :            :          * If recovery has not finished, we should not create a checkpoint.
    8550                 :            :          * Restart point has been updated by xlog redo in case of recovery.
    8551                 :            :          */
    8552         [ +  - ]:          2 :         if (!RecoveryInProgress())
    8553                 :          2 :                 CreateCheckPoint(CHECKPOINT_IS_SHUTDOWN | CHECKPOINT_IMMEDIATE);
    8554                 :            : 
    8555                 :          2 :         ShutdownCLOG();
    8556                 :          2 :         ShutdownSUBTRANS();
    8557                 :          2 :         ShutdownMultiXact();
    8558                 :          2 :         DistributedLog_Shutdown();
    8559                 :            : 
    8560         [ +  - ]:          2 :         ereport(LOG,
    8561                 :            :                         (errmsg("database system is shut down"),
    8562                 :            :                                         errSendAlert(true)));
    8563                 :          2 : }
    8564                 :            : 
    8565                 :            : /*
    8566                 :            :  * Calculate the last segment that we need to retain because of
    8567                 :            :  * keep_wal_segments, by subtracting keep_wal_segments from the passed
    8568                 :            :  * xlog location
    8569                 :            :  */
    8570                 :            : static void
    8571                 :            : CheckKeepWalSegments(XLogRecPtr recptr, uint32 *_logId, uint32 *_logSeg)
    8572                 :            : {
    8573                 :            :         uint32  log;
    8574                 :            :         uint32  seg;
    8575                 :            :         uint32  keep_log;
    8576                 :            :         uint32  keep_seg;
    8577                 :            : 
    8578         [ -  + ]:          6 :         if (keep_wal_segments <= 0)
    8579                 :          0 :                 return;
    8580                 :            : 
    8581                 :          6 :         XLByteToSeg(recptr, log, seg);
    8582                 :            : 
    8583                 :          6 :         keep_seg = keep_wal_segments % XLogSegsPerFile;
    8584                 :          6 :         keep_log = keep_wal_segments / XLogSegsPerFile;
    8585         [ -  + ]:          6 :         ereport(DEBUG1,
    8586                 :            :                         (errmsg("%s: Input %d %d (Keep %d %d) (current %d %d)",
    8587                 :            :                                         PG_FUNCNAME_MACRO, *_logId, *_logSeg, keep_log,
    8588                 :            :                                         keep_seg, log, seg)));
    8589         [ +  - ]:          6 :         if (seg < keep_seg)
    8590                 :            :         {
    8591                 :          6 :                 keep_log += 1;
    8592                 :          6 :                 seg = seg - keep_seg + XLogSegsPerFile;
    8593                 :          6 :         }
    8594                 :            :         else
    8595                 :            :         {
    8596                 :          0 :                 seg = seg - keep_seg;
    8597                 :            :         }
    8598                 :            : 
    8599                 :            :         /* Avoid underflow, don't go below (0,1) */
    8600 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:          6 :         if (log < keep_log || (log == keep_log && seg == 0))
                 [ #  # ]
    8601                 :            :         {
    8602                 :          6 :                 log = 0;
    8603                 :          6 :                 seg = 1;
    8604                 :          6 :         }
    8605                 :            :         else
    8606                 :            :         {
    8607                 :          0 :                 log = log - keep_log;
    8608                 :            :         }
    8609                 :            : 
    8610                 :            :         /* check not to delete WAL segments newer than the calculated segment */
    8611 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:          6 :         if (log < *_logId || (log == *_logId && seg < *_logSeg))
                 [ +  - ]
    8612                 :            :         {
    8613                 :          6 :                 *_logId = log;
    8614                 :          6 :                 *_logSeg = seg;
    8615                 :          6 :         }
    8616                 :            : 
    8617         [ -  + ]:          6 :         ereport(DEBUG1,
    8618                 :            :                         (errmsg("%s: Output %d %d",
    8619                 :            :                                         PG_FUNCNAME_MACRO, *_logId, *_logSeg)));
    8620                 :          6 : }

    8622                 :            : /*
    8623                 :            :  * Log start of a checkpoint.
    8624                 :            :  */
    8625                 :            : static void
    8626                 :            : LogCheckpointStart(int flags)
    8627                 :            : {
    8628                 :          0 :         elog(LOG, "checkpoint starting:%s%s%s%s%s%s",
    8629                 :            :                  (flags & CHECKPOINT_IS_SHUTDOWN) ? " shutdown" : "",
    8630                 :            :                  (flags & CHECKPOINT_IMMEDIATE) ? " immediate" : "",
    8631                 :            :                  (flags & CHECKPOINT_FORCE) ? " force" : "",
    8632                 :            :                  (flags & CHECKPOINT_WAIT) ? " wait" : "",
    8633                 :            :                  (flags & CHECKPOINT_CAUSE_XLOG) ? " xlog" : "",
    8634                 :            :                  (flags & CHECKPOINT_CAUSE_TIME) ? " time" : "");
    8635                 :          0 : }
    8636                 :            : 
    8637                 :            : /*
    8638                 :            :  * Log end of a checkpoint.
    8639                 :            :  */
    8640                 :            : static void
    8641                 :            : LogCheckpointEnd(void)
    8642                 :            : {
    8643                 :            :         long            write_secs,
    8644                 :            :                                 sync_secs,
    8645                 :            :                                 total_secs;
    8646                 :            :         int                     write_usecs,
    8647                 :            :                                 sync_usecs,
    8648                 :            :                                 total_usecs;
    8649                 :            : 
    8650                 :          0 :         CheckpointStats.ckpt_end_t = GetCurrentTimestamp();
    8651                 :            : 
    8652                 :          0 :         TimestampDifference(CheckpointStats.ckpt_start_t,
    8653                 :          0 :                                                 CheckpointStats.ckpt_end_t,
    8654                 :            :                                                 &total_secs, &total_usecs);
    8655                 :            : 
    8656                 :          0 :         TimestampDifference(CheckpointStats.ckpt_write_t,
    8657                 :          0 :                                                 CheckpointStats.ckpt_sync_t,
    8658                 :            :                                                 &write_secs, &write_usecs);
    8659                 :            : 
    8660                 :          0 :         TimestampDifference(CheckpointStats.ckpt_sync_t,
    8661                 :          0 :                                                 CheckpointStats.ckpt_sync_end_t,
    8662                 :            :                                                 &sync_secs, &sync_usecs);
    8663                 :            : 
    8664                 :          0 :         elog(LOG, "checkpoint complete: wrote %d buffers (%.1f%%); "
    8665                 :            :                  "%d transaction log file(s) added, %d removed, %d recycled; "
    8666                 :            :                  "write=%ld.%03d s, sync=%ld.%03d s, total=%ld.%03d s",
    8667                 :            :                  CheckpointStats.ckpt_bufs_written,
    8668                 :            :                  (double) CheckpointStats.ckpt_bufs_written * 100 / NBuffers,
    8669                 :            :                  CheckpointStats.ckpt_segs_added,
    8670                 :            :                  CheckpointStats.ckpt_segs_removed,
    8671                 :            :                  CheckpointStats.ckpt_segs_recycled,
    8672                 :            :                  write_secs, write_usecs / 1000,
    8673                 :            :                  sync_secs, sync_usecs / 1000,
    8674                 :            :                  total_secs, total_usecs / 1000);
    8675                 :          0 : }

    8677                 :            : /*
    8678                 :            :  * Perform a checkpoint --- either during shutdown, or on-the-fly
    8679                 :            :  *
    8680                 :            :  * If force is true, we force a checkpoint regardless of whether any XLOG
    8681                 :            :  * activity has occurred since the last one.
    8682                 :            :  */

    8684                 :            : CreateCheckPoint(int flags)

    8688                 :            : 
    8689                 :         14 :         bool            shutdown = (flags & CHECKPOINT_IS_SHUTDOWN) != 0;
    8690                 :            :         CheckPoint      checkPoint;
    8691                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      recptr;
    8692                 :         14 :         XLogCtlInsert *Insert = &XLogCtl->Insert;
    8693                 :            :         XLogRecData rdata[6];

    8696                 :            :         uint32          freespace;

    8699                 :            :         VirtualTransactionId *vxids;

    8702                 :            : 
    8703 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         14 :         if (shutdown && ControlFile->state == DB_STARTUP)
    8704                 :            :         {
    8705                 :          0 :                 return;
    8706                 :            :         }
    8707                 :            : 
    8708                 :            : #ifdef FAULT_INJECTOR
    8709                 :            :         /* During resync checkpoint has to complete otherwise segment cannot transition into Sync state */
    8710         [ +  - ]:         14 :         if (! resync_to_sync_transition)
    8711                 :            :         {

    8713                 :            :                                                                                    Checkpoint,
    8714                 :            :                                                                                    DDLNotSpecified,
    8715                 :            :                                                                                    "" /* databaseName */,
    8716                 :         14 :                                                                                    "" /* tableName */) == FaultInjectorTypeSkip)
    8717                 :          0 :                         return;  // skip checkpoint
    8718                 :         14 :         }
    8719                 :            : #endif
    8720                 :            : 
    8721                 :            :         /*
    8722                 :            :          * Acquire CheckpointLock to ensure only one checkpoint happens at a time.
    8723                 :            :          * (This is just pro forma, since in the present system structure there is
    8724                 :            :          * only one process that is allowed to issue checkpoints at any given
    8725                 :            :          * time.)
    8726                 :            :          */
    8727                 :         14 :         LWLockAcquire(CheckpointLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
    8728                 :            : 
    8729                 :            :         /*

    8732                 :            :          * Note: because it is possible for log_checkpoints to change while a

         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ +  + ]
    8737                 :         14 :         CheckpointStats.ckpt_start_t = GetCurrentTimestamp();
    8738                 :            : 
    8739         [ -  + ]:         14 :         if (resync_to_sync_transition)
    8740                 :            :         {
    8741                 :          0 :                 LWLockAcquire(MirroredLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
    8742                 :            : 
    8743                 :            :                 /* database transitions to suspended state, IO activity on the segment is suspended */
    8744                 :          0 :                 primaryMirrorSetIOSuspended(TRUE);
    8745                 :            : 
    8746                 :          0 :                 SIMPLE_FAULT_INJECTOR(FileRepTransitionToInSyncBeforeCheckpoint);
    8747                 :          0 :         }

    8749                 :            :         {
    8750                 :            :                 /*
    8751                 :            :                  * Normal case.
    8752                 :            :                  */
    8753 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:         14 :                 MIRRORED_LOCK;
         [ #  # ][ +  - ]
                 [ -  + ]
    8754                 :            :         }
    8755                 :            : 
    8756                 :            :         /*
    8757                 :            :          * Use a critical section to force system panic if we have trouble.
    8758                 :            :          */
    8759         [ -  + ]:         14 :         START_CRIT_SECTION();
    8760                 :            : 
    8761         [ +  - ]:         14 :         if (shutdown)
    8762                 :            :         {
    8763                 :            :                 /*
    8764                 :            :                  * This is an ugly fix to dis-allow changing the pg_control

    8766                 :            :                  *

    8768                 :            :                  */
    8769         [ +  - ]:         14 :                 if (ControlFile->state != DB_IN_STANDBY_PROMOTED
    8770         [ +  - ]:         14 :                         && ControlFile->state != DB_IN_STANDBY_NEW_TLI_SET)
    8771                 :            :                 {
    8772                 :         14 :                         ControlFile->state = DB_SHUTDOWNING;
    8773                 :         14 :                         ControlFile->time = time(NULL);
    8774                 :         14 :                         UpdateControlFile();
    8775                 :         14 :                 }
    8776                 :         14 :         }
    8777                 :            : 
    8778                 :            :         /*
    8779                 :            :          * Let smgr prepare for checkpoint; this has to happen before we determine

    8783                 :         14 :         smgrpreckpt();
    8784                 :            : 
    8785                 :            :         /* Begin filling in the checkpoint WAL record */
    8786 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:         98 :         MemSet(&checkPoint, 0, sizeof(checkPoint));
         [ +  - ][ +  - ]
                 [ +  + ]
    8787                 :         14 :         checkPoint.ThisTimeLineID = ThisTimeLineID;
    8788                 :         14 :         checkPoint.time = time(NULL);
    8789                 :            : 
    8790                 :            :         /*
    8791                 :            :          * The WRITE_PERSISTENT_STATE_ORDERED_LOCK gets these locks:
    8792                 :            :          *    MirroredLock SHARED, and
    8793                 :            :          *    PersistentObjLock EXCLUSIVE.
    8794                 :            :          * as well as set MyProc->inCommit = true.
    8795                 :            :          *
    8796                 :            :          * The READ_PERSISTENT_STATE_ORDERED_LOCK gets this lock:
    8797                 :            :          *    PersistentObjLock SHARED.
    8798                 :            :          *
    8799                 :            :          * They do this to prevent Persistent object changes during checkpoint and
    8800                 :            :          * prevent persistent object reads while writing.  And acquire the MirroredLock
    8801                 :            :          * at a level that blocks DDL during FileRep statechanges...
    8802                 :            :          */
    8803                 :            : 
    8804                 :            :         /*
    8805                 :            :          * We must hold WALInsertLock while examining insert state to determine
    8806                 :            :          * the checkpoint REDO pointer.
    8807                 :            :          */
    8808                 :         14 :         LWLockAcquire(WALInsertLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
    8809                 :            : 
    8810                 :            :         /*
    8811                 :            :          * If this isn't a shutdown or forced checkpoint, and we have not inserted
    8812                 :            :          * any XLOG records since the start of the last checkpoint, skip the
    8813                 :            :          * checkpoint.  The idea here is to avoid inserting duplicate checkpoints
    8814                 :            :          * when the system is idle. That wastes log space, and more importantly it
    8815                 :            :          * exposes us to possible loss of both current and previous checkpoint
    8816                 :            :          * records if the machine crashes just as we're writing the update.
    8817                 :            :          * (Perhaps it'd make even more sense to checkpoint only when the previous
    8818                 :            :          * checkpoint record is in a different xlog page?)
    8819                 :            :          *
    8820                 :            :          * We have to make two tests to determine that nothing has happened since
    8821                 :            :          * the start of the last checkpoint: current insertion point must match
    8822                 :            :          * the end of the last checkpoint record, and its redo pointer must point
    8823                 :            :          * to itself.
    8824                 :            :          */
    8825         [ -  + ]:         14 :         if ((flags & (CHECKPOINT_IS_SHUTDOWN | CHECKPOINT_FORCE)) == 0)
    8826                 :            :         {
    8827                 :            :                 XLogRecPtr      curInsert;
    8828                 :            : 
    8829                 :          0 :                 INSERT_RECPTR(curInsert, Insert, Insert->curridx);
    8830                 :            : #ifdef originalCheckpointChecking
    8831                 :            :                 if (curInsert.xlogid == ControlFile->checkPoint.xlogid &&
    8832                 :            :                         curInsert.xrecoff == ControlFile->checkPoint.xrecoff +
    8833                 :            :                         MAXALIGN(SizeOfXLogRecord + sizeof(CheckPoint)) &&
    8834                 :            :                         ControlFile->checkPoint.xlogid ==
    8835                 :            :                         ControlFile->checkPointCopy.redo.xlogid &&
    8836                 :            :                         ControlFile->checkPoint.xrecoff ==
    8837                 :            :                         ControlFile->checkPointCopy.redo.xrecoff)
    8838                 :            : #else
    8839                 :            :                 /*
    8840                 :            :                  * GP: Modified since the checkpoint record is not fixed length
    8841                 :            :                  * so we keep track of the last checkpoint locations (beginning and
    8842                 :            :                  * end) and use thoe values for comparison.
    8843                 :            :                  */

    8845 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         XLByteEQ(XLogCtl->lastCheckpointLoc,ControlFile->checkPoint) &&
    8846 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         XLByteEQ(curInsert,XLogCtl->lastCheckpointEndLoc) &&
    8847         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         XLByteEQ(ControlFile->checkPoint,ControlFile->checkPointCopy.redo))
    8848                 :            : #endif
    8849                 :            :                 {
    8850                 :          0 :                         LWLockRelease(WALInsertLock);
    8851         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (resync_to_sync_transition)
    8852                 :            :                         {
    8853                 :          0 :                                 LWLockRelease(MirroredLock);
    8854                 :          0 :                         }
    8855                 :            :                         else
    8856                 :            :                         {
    8857                 :            :                                 /*
    8858                 :            :                                  * Normal case.
    8859                 :            :                                  */
    8860 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 MIRRORED_UNLOCK;
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
                 [ #  # ]
    8861                 :            :                         }
    8862                 :          0 :                         LWLockRelease(CheckpointLock);
    8863                 :            : 
    8864         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         END_CRIT_SECTION();
    8865                 :          0 :                         return;
    8866                 :            :                 }
    8867                 :          0 :         }
    8868                 :            : 
    8869                 :            :         /*
    8870                 :            :          * Compute new REDO record ptr = location of next XLOG record.
    8871                 :            :          *
    8872                 :            :          * NB: this is NOT necessarily where the checkpoint record itself will be,
    8873                 :            :          * since other backends may insert more XLOG records while we're off doing
    8874                 :            :          * the buffer flush work.  Those XLOG records are logically after the
    8875                 :            :          * checkpoint, even though physically before it.  Got that?
    8876                 :            :          */

    8878         [ -  + ]:         14 :         if (freespace < SizeOfXLogRecord)
    8879                 :            :         {
    8880                 :          0 :                 (void) AdvanceXLInsertBuffer(false);
    8881                 :            :                 /* OK to ignore update return flag, since we will do flush anyway */
    8882                 :          0 :                 freespace = INSERT_FREESPACE(Insert);
    8883                 :          0 :         }
    8884                 :         14 :         INSERT_RECPTR(checkPoint.redo, Insert, Insert->curridx);
    8885                 :            : 
    8886                 :            :         /*
    8887                 :            :          * Here we update the shared RedoRecPtr for future XLogInsert calls; this
    8888                 :            :          * must be done while holding the insert lock AND the info_lck.
    8889                 :            :          *
    8890                 :            :          * Note: if we fail to complete the checkpoint, RedoRecPtr will be left
    8891                 :            :          * pointing past where it really needs to point.  This is okay; the only
    8892                 :            :          * consequence is that XLogInsert might back up whole buffers that it
    8893                 :            :          * didn't really need to.  We can't postpone advancing RedoRecPtr because
    8894                 :            :          * XLogInserts that happen while we are dumping buffers must assume that
    8895                 :            :          * their buffer changes are not included in the checkpoint.
    8896                 :            :          */
    8897                 :            :         {
    8898                 :            :                 /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */
    8899                 :         14 :                 volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
    8900                 :            : 
    8901         [ -  + ]:         14 :                 SpinLockAcquire(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8902                 :         14 :                 RedoRecPtr = xlogctl->Insert.RedoRecPtr = checkPoint.redo;
    8903                 :         14 :                 SpinLockRelease(&xlogctl->info_lck);
    8904                 :            :         }
    8905                 :            : 
    8906                 :            :         /*
    8907                 :            :          * Now we can release WAL insert lock, allowing other xacts to proceed
    8908                 :            :          * while we are flushing disk buffers.
    8909                 :            :          */
    8910                 :         14 :         LWLockRelease(WALInsertLock);
    8911                 :            : 
    8912                 :            :         /*
    8913                 :            :          * If enabled, log checkpoint start.  We postpone this until now so as not
    8914                 :            :          * to log anything if we decided to skip the checkpoint.
    8915                 :            :          */
    8916         [ -  + ]:         14 :         if (log_checkpoints)
    8917                 :          0 :                 LogCheckpointStart(flags);
    8918                 :            : 
    8919                 :            :         /*
    8920                 :            :          * Before flushing data, we must wait for any transactions that are
    8921                 :            :          * currently in their commit critical sections.  If an xact inserted its
    8922                 :            :          * commit record into XLOG just before the REDO point, then a crash
    8923                 :            :          * restart from the REDO point would not replay that record, which means
    8924                 :            :          * that our flushing had better include the xact's update of pg_clog.  So
    8925                 :            :          * we wait till he's out of his commit critical section before proceeding.
    8926                 :            :          * See notes in RecordTransactionCommit().
    8927                 :            :          *
    8928                 :            :          * Because we've already released WALInsertLock, this test is a bit fuzzy:
    8929                 :            :          * it is possible that we will wait for xacts we didn't really need to
    8930                 :            :          * wait for.  But the delay should be short and it seems better to make
    8931                 :            :          * checkpoint take a bit longer than to hold locks longer than necessary.
    8932                 :            :          * (In fact, the whole reason we have this issue is that xact.c does
    8933                 :            :          * commit record XLOG insertion and clog update as two separate steps
    8934                 :            :          * protected by different locks, but again that seems best on grounds of
    8935                 :            :          * minimizing lock contention.)
    8936                 :            :          *
    8937                 :            :          * A transaction that has not yet set inCommit when we look cannot be at
    8938                 :            :          * risk, since he's not inserted his commit record yet; and one that's
    8939                 :            :          * already cleared it is not at risk either, since he's done fixing clog
    8940                 :            :          * and we will correctly flush the update below.  So we cannot miss any
    8941                 :            :          * xacts we need to wait for.
    8942                 :            :          */
    8943                 :         14 :         vxids = GetVirtualXIDsDelayingChkpt(&nvxids);
    8944         [ -  + ]:         14 :         if (nvxids > 0)
    8945                 :            :         {
    8946                 :          0 :                 do
    8947                 :            :                 {
    8948                 :            :                         /*
    8949                 :            :                          * GPDB needs to AbsorbFsyncRequests() here to avoid deadlock when
    8950                 :            :                          * fsync request queue is full while backend is in commit and
    8951                 :            :                          * performing ForgetRelationFsyncRequests() or
    8952                 :            :                          * ForgetDatabaseFsyncRequests(). Since for GPDB the mdlink
    8953                 :            :                          * happens through persistent tables cleanup, during which
    8954                 :            :                          * inCommit flag is set to avoid checkpoint from happening.
    8955                 :            :                          * PostgreSQL doesn't need this as ForgetRelationFsyncRequests()
    8956                 :            :                          * or ForgetDatabaseFsyncRequests() are not under inCommit=true.
    8957                 :            :                          */
    8958                 :          0 :                         AbsorbFsyncRequests();
    8959                 :          0 :                         pg_usleep(10000L);      /* wait for 10 msec */
    8960         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 } while (HaveVirtualXIDsDelayingChkpt(vxids, nvxids));
    8961                 :          0 :         }
    8962                 :         14 :         pfree(vxids);

    8964                 :            :         /*
    8965                 :            :          * Get the other info we need for the checkpoint record.
    8966                 :            :          */

    8969                 :         14 :         LWLockRelease(XidGenLock);

    8975                 :            :

    8978         [ -  + ]:         14 :         if (!shutdown)
    8979                 :          0 :                 checkPoint.nextOid += ShmemVariableCache->oidCount;

    8992                 :            :         /*

    8997                 :            :          * complete the checkpoint, but there is no reason to force a system

    9006                 :            :         /*
    9007                 :            :          * Now insert the checkpoint record into XLOG.
    9008                 :            :          *
    9009                 :            :          * Here is the locking order and scope:
    9010                 :            :          *
    9011                 :            :          * getDtxCheckPointInfoAndLock (i.e. shmControlLock)
    9012                 :            :          *      READ_PERSISTENT_STATE_ORDERED_LOCK (i.e. PersistentObjLock)
    9013                 :            :          *              mmxlog_append_checkpoint_data
    9014                 :            :          *              XLogInsert
    9015                 :            :          *      READ_PERSISTENT_STATE_ORDERED_UNLOCK
    9016                 :            :          * freeDtxCheckPointInfoAndUnlock
    9017                 :            :          * XLogFlush
    9018                 :            :          *
    9019                 :            :          * We get the PersistentObjLock to prevent Persistent Object writers as
    9020                 :            :          * we collect the Master Mirroring information from mmxlog_append_checkpoint_data()
    9021                 :            :          * until finally after the checkpoint record is inserted into the XLOG to prevent the
    9022                 :            :          * persistent information from changing.
    9023                 :            :          *
    9024                 :            :          * For example, if we don't hold the PersistentObjLock across mmxlog_append_checkpoint_data()
    9025                 :            :          * and XLogInsert(), another xlog activity like drop tablespace could happen in between, which
    9026                 :            :          * might caused wrong behavior when master standby replay checkpoint record.
    9027                 :            :          *
    9028                 :            :          * Master standby replay (mmxlog_read_checkpoint_data) the mmxlog information stored in the checkpoint
    9029                 :            :          * record to recreate those persistent objects like filespace, tablespace, database dir, etc. If those
    9030                 :            :          * objects dropped after checkpoint collected persistent objects information, but before checkpoint
    9031                 :            :          * record write to XLOG, then the standby replay would first drop the object based on mmxlog record,
    9032                 :            :          * then recreated based on the checkpoint record. That will ends-up left behind the directories already
    9033                 :            :          * dropped on the master, break the consistency between the master and the standby.
    9034                 :            :          */

    9293                 :            : /*

    9296                 :            :  * This is the common code shared between regular checkpoints and

    9298                 :            :  */
    9299                 :            : static void
    9300                 :            : CheckPointGuts(XLogRecPtr checkPointRedo, int flags)
    9301                 :            : {
    9302                 :         39 :         CheckPointCLOG();
    9303                 :         39 :         CheckPointSUBTRANS();
    9304                 :         39 :         CheckPointMultiXact();
    9305                 :         39 :         CheckPointChangeTracking();
    9306                 :         39 :         DistributedLog_CheckPoint();
    9307                 :         39 :         CheckPointBuffers(flags);       /* performs all required fsyncs */
    9308                 :            :         /* We deliberately delay 2PC checkpointing as long as possible */
    9309                 :         39 :         CheckPointTwoPhase(checkPointRedo);
    9310                 :         39 : }
    9311                 :            : 
    9312                 :            : static void
    9313                 :            : Checkpoint_RecoveryPass(XLogRecPtr checkPointRedo)
    9314                 :            : {
    9315                 :         20 :         CheckPointGuts(checkPointRedo, CHECKPOINT_IS_SHUTDOWN | CHECKPOINT_IMMEDIATE);
    9316                 :         20 : }
    9317                 :            : 
    9318                 :            : /*
    9319                 :            :  * Set a recovery restart point if appropriate
    9320                 :            :  *
    9321                 :            :  * This is similar to CreateCheckpoint, but is used during WAL recovery
    9322                 :            :  * to establish a point from which recovery can roll forward without
    9323                 :            :  * replaying the entire recovery log.  This function is called each time
    9324                 :            :  * a checkpoint record is read from XLOG; it must determine whether a
    9325                 :            :  * restartpoint is needed or not.
    9326                 :            :  */
    9327                 :            : static void
    9328                 :            : RecoveryRestartPoint(const CheckPoint *checkPoint)
    9329                 :            : {
    9330                 :            : //      int                     elapsed_secs;
    9331                 :            :         int                     rmid;
    9332                 :          5 :         uint32 _logId = 0;
    9333                 :          5 :         uint32 _logSeg = 0;
    9334                 :            : 
    9335                 :            :         /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */

    9337                 :            : 
    9338                 :            :         /*
    9339                 :            :          * Do nothing if the elapsed time since the last restartpoint is less than
    9340                 :            :          * half of checkpoint_timeout.  (We use a value less than
    9341                 :            :          * checkpoint_timeout so that variations in the timing of checkpoints on
    9342                 :            :          * the master, or speed of transmission of WAL segments to a slave, won't
    9343                 :            :          * make the slave skip a restartpoint once it's synced with the master.)
    9344                 :            :          * Checking true elapsed time keeps us from doing restartpoints too often
    9345                 :            :          * while rapidly scanning large amounts of WAL.
    9346                 :            :          */

    9440                 :            : /*
    9441                 :            :  * Write a NEXTOID log record
    9442                 :            :  */
    9443                 :            : void
    9444                 :            : XLogPutNextOid(Oid nextOid)
    9445                 :            : {
    9446                 :            :         XLogRecData rdata;
    9447                 :            : 
    9448                 :        124 :         rdata.data = (char *) (&nextOid);
    9449                 :        124 :         rdata.len = sizeof(Oid);
    9450                 :        124 :         rdata.buffer = InvalidBuffer;
    9451                 :        124 :         rdata.next = NULL;
    9452                 :        124 :         (void) XLogInsert(RM_XLOG_ID, XLOG_NEXTOID, &rdata);
    9453                 :            : 
    9454                 :            :         /*
    9455                 :            :          * We need not flush the NEXTOID record immediately, because any of the
    9456                 :            :          * just-allocated OIDs could only reach disk as part of a tuple insert or
    9457                 :            :          * update that would have its own XLOG record that must follow the NEXTOID
    9458                 :            :          * record.      Therefore, the standard buffer LSN interlock applied to those
    9459                 :            :          * records will ensure no such OID reaches disk before the NEXTOID record
    9460                 :            :          * does.
    9461                 :            :          *
    9462                 :            :          * Note, however, that the above statement only covers state "within" the
    9463                 :            :          * database.  When we use a generated OID as a file or directory name, we
    9464                 :            :          * are in a sense violating the basic WAL rule, because that filesystem
    9465                 :            :          * change may reach disk before the NEXTOID WAL record does.  The impact
    9466                 :            :          * of this is that if a database crash occurs immediately afterward, we
    9467                 :            :          * might after restart re-generate the same OID and find that it conflicts
    9468                 :            :          * with the leftover file or directory.  But since for safety's sake we
    9469                 :            :          * always loop until finding a nonconflicting filename, this poses no real
    9470                 :            :          * problem in practice. See pgsql-hackers discussion 27-Sep-2006.
    9471                 :            :          */
    9472                 :        124 : }
    9473                 :            : 
    9474                 :            : /*
    9475                 :            :  * Write a NEXTRELFILENODE log record similar to XLogPutNextOid
    9476                 :            :  */

    9484                 :         53 :         rdata.buffer = InvalidBuffer;
    9485                 :         53 :         rdata.next = NULL;

    9487                 :         53 : }
    9488                 :            : 
    9489                 :            : /*
    9490                 :            :  * Write an XLOG SWITCH record.
    9491                 :            :  *
    9492                 :            :  * Here we just blindly issue an XLogInsert request for the record.
    9493                 :            :  * All the magic happens inside XLogInsert.
    9494                 :            :  *
    9495                 :            :  * The return value is either the end+1 address of the switch record,
    9496                 :            :  * or the end+1 address of the prior segment if we did not need to
    9497                 :            :  * write a switch record because we are already at segment start.

    9504                 :            : 
    9505                 :            :         /* XLOG SWITCH, alone among xlog record types, has no data */
    9506                 :          0 :         rdata.buffer = InvalidBuffer;
    9507                 :          0 :         rdata.data = NULL;
    9508                 :          0 :         rdata.len = 0;
    9509                 :          0 :         rdata.next = NULL;
    9510                 :            : 
    9511                 :          0 :         RecPtr = XLogInsert(RM_XLOG_ID, XLOG_SWITCH, &rdata);
    9512                 :            : 
    9513                 :          0 :         return RecPtr;
    9514                 :            : }

    9744                 :            :         {

    9787                 :            :         {
    9788                 :          0 :                 new_sync_method = SYNC_METHOD_FSYNC;
    9789                 :          0 :                 new_sync_bit = 0;
    9790                 :          0 :         }
    9791                 :            : #ifdef HAVE_FSYNC_WRITETHROUGH
    9792         [ -  + ]:      22309 :         else if (pg_strcasecmp(method, "fsync_writethrough") == 0)
    9793                 :            :         {
    9794                 :          0 :                 new_sync_method = SYNC_METHOD_FSYNC_WRITETHROUGH;
    9795                 :          0 :                 new_sync_bit = 0;
    9796                 :          0 :         }
    9797                 :            : #endif
    9798                 :            : #ifdef HAVE_FDATASYNC
    9799         [ -  + ]:      22309 :         else if (pg_strcasecmp(method, "fdatasync") == 0)
    9800                 :            :         {
    9801                 :          0 :                 new_sync_method = SYNC_METHOD_FDATASYNC;
    9802                 :          0 :                 new_sync_bit = 0;
    9803                 :          0 :         }
    9804                 :            : #endif
    9805                 :            : #ifdef OPEN_SYNC_FLAG
    9806         [ -  + ]:      22309 :         else if (pg_strcasecmp(method, "open_sync") == 0)
    9807                 :            :         {
    9808                 :          0 :                 new_sync_method = SYNC_METHOD_OPEN;

    9811                 :            : #endif

    9841                 :            :                                                                 openLogId, openLogSeg)));

    9843                 :          0 :                                 XLogFileClose();
    9844                 :          0 :                 }
    9845                 :          0 :                 sync_method = new_sync_method;
    9846                 :          0 :                 open_sync_bit = new_sync_bit;

    9849                 :      22309 :         return method;
