    9851                 :            : 
    9852                 :            : /*
    9853                 :            :  * Issue appropriate kind of fsync (if any) for an XLOG output file.
    9854                 :            :  *
    9855                 :            :  * 'fd' is a file descriptor for the XLOG file to be fsync'd.
    9856                 :            :  * 'log' and 'seg' are for error reporting purposes.
    9857                 :            :  */
    9858                 :            : void
    9859                 :            : issue_xlog_fsync(int fd, uint32 log, uint32 seg)
    9860                 :            : {
    9861   [ #  #  #  #  :          0 :         switch (sync_method)
                      # ]
    9862                 :            :         {
    9863                 :            :                 case SYNC_METHOD_FSYNC:
    9864         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (pg_fsync_no_writethrough(fd) != 0)
    9865         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(PANIC,
    9866                 :            :                                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    9867                 :            :                                                  errmsg("could not fsync log file %u, segment %u: %m",
    9868                 :            :                                                                 log, seg)));
    9869                 :          0 :                         break;
    9870                 :            : #ifdef HAVE_FSYNC_WRITETHROUGH
    9871                 :            :                 case SYNC_METHOD_FSYNC_WRITETHROUGH:
    9872         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (pg_fsync_writethrough(fd) != 0)
    9873         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(PANIC,
    9874                 :            :                                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    9875                 :            :                                                  errmsg("could not fsync write-through log file %u, segment %u: %m",
    9876                 :            :                                                                 log, seg)));
    9877                 :          0 :                         break;
    9878                 :            : #endif
    9879                 :            : #ifdef HAVE_FDATASYNC
    9880                 :            :                 case SYNC_METHOD_FDATASYNC:
    9881         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (pg_fdatasync(fd) != 0)
    9882         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(PANIC,
    9883                 :            :                                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    9884                 :            :                                         errmsg("could not fdatasync log file %u, segment %u: %m",
    9885                 :            :                                                    log, seg)));
    9886                 :          0 :                         break;
    9887                 :            : #endif
    9888                 :            :                 case SYNC_METHOD_OPEN:
    9889                 :            : //              case SYNC_METHOD_OPEN_DSYNC:
    9890                 :            :                         /* write synced it already */
    9891                 :          0 :                         break;
    9892                 :            :                 default:
    9893                 :          0 :                         elog(PANIC, "unrecognized wal_sync_method: %d", sync_method);
    9894                 :          0 :                         break;
    9895                 :            :         }
    9896                 :          0 : }
    9897                 :            : 
    9898                 :            : /*
    9899                 :            :  * do_pg_start_backup is the workhorse of the user-visible pg_start_backup()
    9900                 :            :  * function. It creates the necessary starting checkpoint and constructs the
    9901                 :            :  * backup label file.
    9902                 :            :  *
    9903                 :            :  * There are two kind of backups: exclusive and non-exclusive. An exclusive
    9904                 :            :  * backup is started with pg_start_backup(), and there can be only one active
    9905                 :            :  * at a time. The backup label file of an exclusive backup is written to
    9906                 :            :  * $PGDATA/backup_label, and it is removed by pg_stop_backup().
    9907                 :            :  *
    9908                 :            :  * A non-exclusive backup is used for the streaming base backups (see
    9909                 :            :  * src/backend/replication/basebackup.c). The difference to exclusive backups
    9910                 :            :  * is that the backup label file is not written to disk. Instead, its would-be
    9911                 :            :  * contents are returned in *labelfile, and the caller is responsible for
    9912                 :            :  * including it in the backup archive as 'backup_label'. There can be many
    9913                 :            :  * non-exclusive backups active at the same time, and they don't conflict
    9914                 :            :  * with an exclusive backup either.
    9915                 :            :  *
    9916                 :            :  * Every successfully started non-exclusive backup must be stopped by calling
    9917                 :            :  * do_pg_stop_backup() or do_pg_abort_backup().
    9918                 :            :  */
    9919                 :            : XLogRecPtr
    9920                 :            : do_pg_start_backup(const char *backupidstr, bool fast, char **labelfile)
    9921                 :            : {
    9922                 :          0 :         bool            exclusive = (labelfile == NULL);
    9923                 :          0 :         bool            backup_started_in_recovery = false;
    9924                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      checkpointloc;
    9925                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      startpoint;
    9926                 :            :         pg_time_t       stamp_time;
    9927                 :            :         char            strfbuf[128];
    9928                 :            :         char            xlogfilename[MAXFNAMELEN];
    9929                 :            :         uint32          _logId;
    9930                 :            :         uint32          _logSeg;
    9931                 :            :         struct stat stat_buf;
    9932                 :            :         FILE       *fp;
    9933                 :            :         StringInfoData labelfbuf;
    9934                 :            : 
    9935                 :            :         /* base backup in recovery mode not currently supported */
    9936                 :          0 :         backup_started_in_recovery = false;
    9937                 :            : 
    9938         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!superuser())
    9939         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    9940                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
    9941                 :            :                    errmsg("must be superuser or replication role to run a backup")));
    9942                 :            : 
    9943         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (strlen(backupidstr) > MAXPGPATH)
    9944         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    9945                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
    9946                 :            :                                  errmsg("backup label too long (max %d bytes)",
    9947                 :            :                                                 MAXPGPATH)));
    9948                 :            : 
    9949                 :            :         /*
    9950                 :            :          * Mark backup active in shared memory.  We must do full-page WAL writes
    9951                 :            :          * during an on-line backup even if not doing so at other times, because
    9952                 :            :          * it's quite possible for the backup dump to obtain a "torn" (partially
    9953                 :            :          * written) copy of a database page if it reads the page concurrently with
    9954                 :            :          * our write to the same page.  This can be fixed as long as the first
    9955                 :            :          * write to the page in the WAL sequence is a full-page write. Hence, we
    9956                 :            :          * turn on forcePageWrites and then force a CHECKPOINT, to ensure there
    9957                 :            :          * are no dirty pages in shared memory that might get dumped while the
    9958                 :            :          * backup is in progress without having a corresponding WAL record.  (Once
    9959                 :            :          * the backup is complete, we need not force full-page writes anymore,
    9960                 :            :          * since we expect that any pages not modified during the backup interval
    9961                 :            :          * must have been correctly captured by the backup.)
    9962                 :            :          *
    9963                 :            :          * Note that forcePageWrites has no effect during an online backup from
    9964                 :            :          * the standby.
    9965                 :            :          *
    9966                 :            :          * We must hold WALInsertLock to change the value of forcePageWrites, to
    9967                 :            :          * ensure adequate interlocking against XLogInsert().
    9968                 :            :          */
    9969                 :          0 :         LWLockAcquire(WALInsertLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
    9970         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (exclusive)
    9971                 :            :         {
    9972         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (XLogCtl->Insert.exclusiveBackup)
    9973                 :            :                 {
    9974                 :          0 :                         LWLockRelease(WALInsertLock);
    9975         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    9976                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
    9977                 :            :                                          errmsg("a backup is already in progress"),
    9978                 :            :                                          errhint("Run pg_stop_backup() and try again.")));
    9979                 :          0 :                 }
    9980                 :          0 :                 XLogCtl->Insert.exclusiveBackup = true;
    9981                 :          0 :         }
    9982                 :            :         else
    9983                 :          0 :                 XLogCtl->Insert.nonExclusiveBackups++;
    9984                 :          0 :         XLogCtl->Insert.forcePageWrites = true;
    9985                 :          0 :         LWLockRelease(WALInsertLock);
    9986                 :            : 
    9987                 :            :         /* Ensure we release forcePageWrites if fail below */
    9988         [ #  # ]:          0 :         PG_ENSURE_ERROR_CLEANUP(pg_start_backup_callback, (Datum) BoolGetDatum(exclusive));
    9989                 :            :         {
    9990                 :          0 :                 bool            gotUniqueStartpoint = false;
    9991                 :            : 
    9992                 :            :                 /*
    9993                 :            :                  * Force an XLOG file switch before the checkpoint, to ensure that the
    9994                 :            :                  * WAL segment the checkpoint is written to doesn't contain pages with
    9995                 :            :                  * old timeline IDs.  That would otherwise happen if you called
    9996                 :            :                  * pg_start_backup() right after restoring from a PITR archive: the
    9997                 :            :                  * first WAL segment containing the startup checkpoint has pages in
    9998                 :            :                  * the beginning with the old timeline ID.      That can cause trouble at
    9999                 :            :                  * recovery: we won't have a history file covering the old timeline if
   10000                 :            :                  * pg_xlog directory was not included in the base backup and the WAL
   10001                 :            :                  * archive was cleared too before starting the backup.
   10002                 :            :                  *
   10003                 :            :                  * This also ensures that we have emitted a WAL page header that has
   10004                 :            :                  * XLP_BKP_REMOVABLE off before we emit the checkpoint record.
   10005                 :            :                  * Therefore, if a WAL archiver (such as pglesslog) is trying to
   10006                 :            :                  * compress out removable backup blocks, it won't remove any that
   10007                 :            :                  * occur after this point.
   10008                 :            :                  *
   10009                 :            :                  * During recovery, we skip forcing XLOG file switch, which means that
   10010                 :            :                  * the backup taken during recovery is not available for the special
   10011                 :            :                  * recovery case described above.
   10012                 :            :                  */
   10013         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!backup_started_in_recovery)
   10014                 :          0 :                         RequestXLogSwitch();
   10015                 :            : 
   10016                 :          0 :                 do
   10017                 :            :                 {
   10018                 :            :                         /*
   10019                 :            :                          * Force a CHECKPOINT.  Aside from being necessary to prevent torn
   10020                 :            :                          * page problems, this guarantees that two successive backup runs
   10021                 :            :                          * will have different checkpoint positions and hence different
   10022                 :            :                          * history file names, even if nothing happened in between.
   10023                 :            :                          *
   10024                 :            :                          * During recovery, establish a restartpoint if possible. We use
   10025                 :            :                          * the last restartpoint as the backup starting checkpoint. This
   10026                 :            :                          * means that two successive backup runs can have same checkpoint
   10027                 :            :                          * positions.
   10028                 :            :                          *
   10029                 :            :                          * Since the fact that we are executing do_pg_start_backup()
   10030                 :            :                          * during recovery means that checkpointer is running, we can use
   10031                 :            :                          * RequestCheckpoint() to establish a restartpoint.
   10032                 :            :                          *
   10033                 :            :                          * We use CHECKPOINT_IMMEDIATE only if requested by user (via
   10034                 :            :                          * passing fast = true).  Otherwise this can take awhile.
   10035                 :            :                          */
   10036                 :          0 :                         RequestCheckpoint(CHECKPOINT_FORCE | CHECKPOINT_WAIT |
   10037                 :          0 :                                                           (fast ? CHECKPOINT_IMMEDIATE : 0));
   10038                 :            : 
   10039                 :            :                         /*
   10040                 :            :                          * Now we need to fetch the checkpoint record location, and also
   10041                 :            :                          * its REDO pointer.  The oldest point in WAL that would be needed
   10042                 :            :                          * to restore starting from the checkpoint is precisely the REDO
   10043                 :            :                          * pointer.
   10044                 :            :                          */
   10045                 :          0 :                         LWLockAcquire(ControlFileLock, LW_SHARED);
   10046                 :          0 :                         checkpointloc = ControlFile->checkPoint;
   10047                 :          0 :                         startpoint = ControlFile->checkPointCopy.redo;
   10048                 :          0 :                         LWLockRelease(ControlFileLock);
   10049                 :            : 
   10050                 :            :                         /*
   10051                 :            :                          * If two base backups are started at the same time (in WAL sender
   10052                 :            :                          * processes), we need to make sure that they use different
   10053                 :            :                          * checkpoints as starting locations, because we use the starting
   10054                 :            :                          * WAL location as a unique identifier for the base backup in the
   10055                 :            :                          * end-of-backup WAL record and when we write the backup history
   10056                 :            :                          * file. Perhaps it would be better generate a separate unique ID
   10057                 :            :                          * for each backup instead of forcing another checkpoint, but
   10058                 :            :                          * taking a checkpoint right after another is not that expensive
   10059                 :            :                          * either because only few buffers have been dirtied yet.
   10060                 :            :                          */
   10061                 :          0 :                         LWLockAcquire(WALInsertLock, LW_SHARED);
   10062 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (XLByteLT(XLogCtl->Insert.lastBackupStart, startpoint))
                 [ #  # ]
   10063                 :            :                         {
   10064                 :          0 :                                 XLogCtl->Insert.lastBackupStart = startpoint;
   10065                 :          0 :                                 gotUniqueStartpoint = true;
   10066                 :          0 :                         }
   10067                 :          0 :                         LWLockRelease(WALInsertLock);
   10068         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 } while (!gotUniqueStartpoint);
   10069                 :            : 
   10070                 :          0 :                 XLByteToSeg(startpoint, _logId, _logSeg);
   10071                 :          0 :                 XLogFileName(xlogfilename, ThisTimeLineID, _logId, _logSeg);
   10072                 :            : 
   10073                 :            :                 /*
   10074                 :            :                  * Construct backup label file
   10075                 :            :                  */
   10076                 :          0 :                 initStringInfo(&labelfbuf);
   10077                 :            : 
   10078                 :            :                 /* Use the log timezone here, not the session timezone */
   10079                 :          0 :                 stamp_time = (pg_time_t) time(NULL);
   10080                 :          0 :                 pg_strftime(strfbuf, sizeof(strfbuf),
   10081                 :            :                                         "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z",
   10082                 :          0 :                                         pg_localtime(&stamp_time, log_timezone));
   10083                 :          0 :                 appendStringInfo(&labelfbuf, "START WAL LOCATION: %X/%X (file %s)\n",
   10084                 :          0 :                                                  startpoint.xlogid, startpoint.xrecoff, xlogfilename);
   10085                 :          0 :                 appendStringInfo(&labelfbuf, "CHECKPOINT LOCATION: %X/%X\n",
   10086                 :          0 :                                                  checkpointloc.xlogid, checkpointloc.xrecoff);
   10087                 :          0 :                 appendStringInfo(&labelfbuf, "BACKUP METHOD: %s\n",
   10088                 :          0 :                                                  exclusive ? "pg_start_backup" : "streamed");
   10089                 :          0 :                 appendStringInfo(&labelfbuf, "BACKUP FROM: %s\n",
   10090                 :          0 :                                                  backup_started_in_recovery ? "standby" : "master");
   10091                 :          0 :                 appendStringInfo(&labelfbuf, "START TIME: %s\n", strfbuf);
   10092                 :          0 :                 appendStringInfo(&labelfbuf, "LABEL: %s\n", backupidstr);
   10093                 :            : 
   10094         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 elogif(debug_basebackup, LOG, "basebackup label file --\n%s", labelfbuf.data);
   10095                 :            : 
   10096                 :            :                 /*
   10097                 :            :                  * Okay, write the file, or return its contents to caller.
   10098                 :            :                  */
   10099         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (exclusive)
   10100                 :            :                 {
   10101                 :            :                         /*
   10102                 :            :                          * Check for existing backup label --- implies a backup is already
   10103                 :            :                          * running.  (XXX given that we checked exclusiveBackup above,
   10104                 :            :                          * maybe it would be OK to just unlink any such label file?)
   10105                 :            :                          */
   10106         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (stat(BACKUP_LABEL_FILE, &stat_buf) != 0)
   10107                 :            :                         {
   10108         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (errno != ENOENT)
   10109         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         ereport(ERROR,
   10110                 :            :                                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),
   10111                 :            :                                                          errmsg("could not stat file \"%s\": %m",
   10112                 :            :                                                                         BACKUP_LABEL_FILE)));
   10113                 :          0 :                         }
   10114                 :            :                         else
   10115         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
   10116                 :            :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
   10117                 :            :                                                  errmsg("a backup is already in progress"),
   10118                 :            :                                                  errhint("If you're sure there is no backup in progress, remove file \"%s\" and try again.",
   10119                 :            :                                                                  BACKUP_LABEL_FILE)));
   10120                 :            : 
   10121                 :          0 :                         fp = AllocateFile(BACKUP_LABEL_FILE, "w");
   10122                 :            : 
   10123         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (!fp)
   10124         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
   10125                 :            :                                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
   10126                 :            :                                                  errmsg("could not create file \"%s\": %m",
   10127                 :            :                                                                 BACKUP_LABEL_FILE)));

   10129         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 fflush(fp) != 0 ||

   10134                 :            :                                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),

   10138                 :          0 :                 }

   10612                 :            : /*
   10613                 :            :  * read_backup_label: check to see if a backup_label file is present
   10614                 :            :  *
   10615                 :            :  * If we see a backup_label during recovery, we assume that we are recovering
   10616                 :            :  * from a backup dump file, and we therefore roll forward from the checkpoint
   10617                 :            :  * identified by the label file, NOT what pg_control says.      This avoids the
   10618                 :            :  * problem that pg_control might have been archived one or more checkpoints
   10619                 :            :  * later than the start of the dump, and so if we rely on it as the start
   10620                 :            :  * point, we will fail to restore a consistent database state.
   10621                 :            :  *
   10622                 :            :  * Returns TRUE if a backup_label was found (and fills the checkpoint
   10623                 :            :  * location and its REDO location into *checkPointLoc and RedoStartLSN,
   10624                 :            :  * respectively); returns FALSE if not. If this backup_label came from a
   10625                 :            :  * streamed backup, *backupEndRequired is set to TRUE.
   10626                 :            :  */
   10627                 :            : static bool
   10628                 :            : read_backup_label(XLogRecPtr *checkPointLoc, bool *backupEndRequired)
   10629                 :            : {
   10630                 :            :         char            startxlogfilename[MAXFNAMELEN];
   10631                 :            :         TimeLineID      tli;
   10632                 :            :         FILE       *lfp;
   10633                 :            :         char            ch;
   10634                 :            :         char            backuptype[20];
   10635                 :            :         char            backupfrom[20];
   10636                 :            : 
   10637                 :         12 :         *backupEndRequired = false;
   10638                 :            : 
   10639                 :            :         /*
   10640                 :            :          * See if label file is present
   10641                 :            :          */
   10642                 :         12 :         lfp = AllocateFile(BACKUP_LABEL_FILE, "r");
   10643         [ +  - ]:         12 :         if (!lfp)
   10644                 :            :         {
   10645         [ -  + ]:         12 :                 if (errno != ENOENT)
   10646         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(FATAL,
   10647                 :            :                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),
   10648                 :            :                                          errmsg("could not read file \"%s\": %m",
   10649                 :            :                                                         BACKUP_LABEL_FILE)));
   10650                 :         12 :                 return false;                   /* it's not there, all is fine */
   10651                 :            :         }
   10652                 :            : 
   10653                 :            :         /*
   10654                 :            :          * Read and parse the START WAL LOCATION, CHECKPOINT and BACKUP_METHOD
   10655                 :            :          * lines (this code is pretty crude, but we are not expecting any variability
   10656                 :            :          * in the file format).
   10657                 :            :          */
   10658         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (fscanf(lfp, "START WAL LOCATION: %X/%X (file %08X%16s)%c",
   10659                 :            :                            &RedoStartLSN.xlogid, &RedoStartLSN.xrecoff, &tli,
   10660 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                            startxlogfilename, &ch) != 5 || ch != '\n')
   10661         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(FATAL,
   10662                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
   10663                 :            :                                  errmsg("invalid data in file \"%s\"", BACKUP_LABEL_FILE)));
   10664                 :            : 
   10665         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (fscanf(lfp, "CHECKPOINT LOCATION: %X/%X%c",
   10666                 :          0 :                            &checkPointLoc->xlogid, &checkPointLoc->xrecoff,
   10667         [ #  # ]:          0 :                            &ch) != 3 || ch != '\n')
   10668         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(FATAL,
   10669                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
   10670                 :            :                                  errmsg("invalid data in file \"%s\"", BACKUP_LABEL_FILE)));
   10671                 :            : 
   10672         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (fscanf(lfp, "BACKUP METHOD: %19s\n", backuptype) == 1)
   10673                 :            :         {
   10674                 :            :                 /* Streaming backup method is only supported */
   10675         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (strcmp(backuptype, "streamed") == 0)
   10676                 :          0 :                         *backupEndRequired = true;
   10677                 :            :                 else
   10678         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(FATAL,
   10679                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
   10680                 :            :                                          errmsg("invalid data in file \"%s\"", BACKUP_LABEL_FILE)));
   10681                 :            : 
   10682                 :          0 :         }
   10683                 :            : 
   10684         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (fscanf(lfp, "BACKUP FROM: %19s\n", backupfrom) == 1)
   10685                 :            :         {
   10686                 :            :                 /* Backup from standby is not supported */
   10687         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (strcmp(backupfrom, "master") != 0)
   10688         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(FATAL,
   10689                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
   10690                 :            :                                          errmsg("invalid data in file \"%s\"", BACKUP_LABEL_FILE)));
   10691                 :          0 :         }
   10692                 :            : 
   10693 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (ferror(lfp) || FreeFile(lfp))
   10694         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(FATAL,
   10695                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
   10696                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not read file \"%s\": %m",
   10697                 :            :                                                 BACKUP_LABEL_FILE)));
   10698                 :            : 
   10699                 :          0 :         return true;
   10700                 :         12 : }

   10702                 :            : /*
   10703                 :            :  * Get latest redo apply position.
   10704                 :            :  *
   10705                 :            :  * Optionally, returns the current recovery target timeline. Callers not
   10706                 :            :  * interested in that may pass NULL for targetTLI.

   10710                 :            : XLogRecPtr
   10711                 :            : GetXLogReplayRecPtr(TimeLineID *targetTLI)
   10712                 :            : {
   10713                 :            :         /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */
   10714                 :          0 :         volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
   10715                 :            :         XLogRecPtr      recptr;
   10716                 :            : 
   10717         [ #  # ]:          0 :         SpinLockAcquire(&xlogctl->info_lck);

   10719         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (targetTLI)
   10720                 :          0 :                 *targetTLI = xlogctl->RecoveryTargetTLI;
   10721                 :          0 :         SpinLockRelease(&xlogctl->info_lck);
   10722                 :            : 
   10723                 :          0 :         return recptr;
   10724                 :            : }
   10725                 :            : 
   10726                 :            : /*
   10727                 :            :  * Get current standby flush position, ie, the last WAL position

   10740                 :          0 :         replayPtr = GetXLogReplayRecPtr(targetTLI);
   10741                 :            : 
   10742 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (XLByteLT(receivePtr, replayPtr))
                 [ #  # ]
   10743                 :          0 :                 return replayPtr;
   10744                 :            :         else
   10745                 :          0 :                 return receivePtr;
   10746                 :          0 : }
   10747                 :            : 
   10748                 :            : /*
   10749                 :            :  * GetRecoveryTargetTLI - get the current recovery target timeline ID
   10750                 :            :  */
   10751                 :            : TimeLineID
   10752                 :            : GetRecoveryTargetTLI(void)
   10753                 :            : {
   10754                 :            :         /* use volatile pointer to prevent code rearrangement */
   10755                 :          0 :         volatile XLogCtlData *xlogctl = XLogCtl;
   10756                 :            :         TimeLineID result;
   10757                 :            : 
   10758         [ #  # ]:          0 :         SpinLockAcquire(&xlogctl->info_lck);

   10760                 :          0 :         SpinLockRelease(&xlogctl->info_lck);

   10936                 :            : /* ------------------------------------------------------
   10937                 :            :  *  Startup Process main entry point and signal handlers
   10938                 :            :  * ------------------------------------------------------
   10939                 :            :  */
   10940                 :            : 
   10941                 :            : /*
   10942                 :            :  * startupproc_quickdie() occurs when signalled SIGQUIT by the postmaster.
   10943                 :            :  *
   10944                 :            :  * Some backend has bought the farm,
   10945                 :            :  * so we need to stop what we're doing and exit.
   10946                 :            :  */
   10947                 :            : static void
   10948                 :            : startupproc_quickdie(SIGNAL_ARGS __attribute__((unused)))
   10949                 :            : {
   10950                 :          0 :         PG_SETMASK(&BlockSig);
   10951                 :            : 
   10952                 :            :         /*
   10953                 :            :          * We DO NOT want to run proc_exit() callbacks -- we're here because
   10954                 :            :          * shared memory may be corrupted, so we don't want to try to clean up our
   10955                 :            :          * transaction.  Just nail the windows shut and get out of town.  Now that
   10956                 :            :          * there's an atexit callback to prevent third-party code from breaking
   10957                 :            :          * things by calling exit() directly, we have to reset the callbacks
   10958                 :            :          * explicitly to make this work as intended.
   10959                 :            :          */
   10960                 :          0 :         on_exit_reset();
   10961                 :            : 
   10962                 :            :         /*
   10963                 :            :          * Note we do exit(2) not exit(0).      This is to force the postmaster into a
   10964                 :            :          * system reset cycle if some idiot DBA sends a manual SIGQUIT to a random
   10965                 :            :          * backend.  This is necessary precisely because we don't clean up our
   10966                 :            :          * shared memory state.  (The "dead man switch" mechanism in pmsignal.c
   10967                 :            :          * should ensure the postmaster sees this as a crash, too, but no harm in
   10968                 :            :          * being doubly sure.)
   10969                 :            :          */
   10970                 :          0 :         exit(2);
   10971                 :          0 : }
   10972                 :            : 
   10973                 :            : /* SIGUSR2: set flag to finish recovery */
   10974                 :            : static void
   10975                 :            : StartupProcTriggerHandler(SIGNAL_ARGS)
   10976                 :            : {
   10977                 :          0 :         int                     save_errno = errno;
   10978                 :            : 
   10979                 :          0 :         WakeupRecovery();
   10980                 :            : 
   10981                 :          0 :         errno = save_errno;
   10982                 :          0 : }
   10983                 :            : 
   10984                 :            : /* SIGHUP: set flag to re-read config file at next convenient time */
   10985                 :            : static void
   10986                 :            : StartupProcSigHupHandler(SIGNAL_ARGS)
   10987                 :            : {
   10988                 :          0 :         int                     save_errno = errno;
   10989                 :            : 
   10990                 :          0 :         got_SIGHUP = true;
   10991                 :          0 :         WakeupRecovery();
   10992                 :            : 
   10993                 :          0 :         errno = save_errno;
   10994                 :          0 : }
   10995                 :            : 
   10996                 :            : /* SIGTERM: set flag to abort redo and exit */
   10997                 :            : static void
   10998                 :            : StartupProcShutdownHandler(SIGNAL_ARGS)
   10999                 :            : {
   11000                 :          0 :         int                     save_errno = errno;
   11001                 :            : 
   11002         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (in_restore_command)
   11003                 :          0 :                 proc_exit(1);
   11004                 :            :         else
   11005                 :          0 :                 shutdown_requested = true;
   11006                 :          0 :         WakeupRecovery();
   11007                 :            : 
   11008                 :          0 :         errno = save_errno;
   11009                 :          0 : }
   11010                 :            : 
   11011                 :            : /* Handle SIGHUP and SIGTERM signals of startup process */

   11015                 :            :         /*
   11016                 :            :          * Check if we were requested to re-read config file.

   11018         [ -  + ]:    6360680 :         if (got_SIGHUP)
   11019                 :            :         {
   11020                 :          0 :                 got_SIGHUP = false;
   11021                 :          0 :                 ProcessConfigFile(PGC_SIGHUP);
   11022                 :          0 :         }
   11023                 :            : 
   11024                 :            :         /*
   11025                 :            :          * Check if we were requested to exit without finishing recovery.
   11026                 :            :          */
   11027         [ -  + ]:    6360680 :         if (shutdown_requested)
   11028                 :          0 :                 proc_exit(1);
   11029                 :            : 
   11030                 :            :         /*
   11031                 :            :          * Emergency bailout if postmaster has died.  This is to avoid the
   11032                 :            :          * necessity for manual cleanup of all postmaster children.
   11033                 :            :          */
   11034 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:    6360680 :         if (IsUnderPostmaster && !PostmasterIsAlive(true))

   11036                 :    6360680 : }

   11038                 :            : static void
   11039                 :            : HandleCrash(SIGNAL_ARGS)

   11045                 :            :      *      signal if needed
   11046                 :            :      */
   11047                 :          0 :     StandardHandlerForSigillSigsegvSigbus_OnMainThread("a startup process", PASS_SIGNAL_ARGS);
   11048                 :          0 : }

   11050                 :            : /* Main entry point for startup process */
   11051                 :            : void
   11052                 :            : StartupProcessMain(int passNum)
   11053                 :            : {
   11054                 :         44 :         am_startup = true;
   11055                 :            :         /*
   11056                 :            :          * If possible, make this process a group leader, so that the postmaster
   11057                 :            :          * can signal any child processes too.
   11058                 :            :          */
   11059                 :            : #ifdef HAVE_SETSID
   11060         [ -  + ]:         44 :         if (setsid() < 0)
   11061                 :          0 :                 elog(FATAL, "setsid() failed: %m");
   11062                 :            : #endif
   11063                 :            : 
   11064                 :            :         /*
   11065                 :            :          * Properly accept or ignore signals the postmaster might send us
   11066                 :            :          */
   11067                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGHUP, StartupProcSigHupHandler);      /* reload config file */
   11068                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGINT, SIG_IGN);                                      /* ignore query cancel */
   11069                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGTERM, StartupProcShutdownHandler); /* request shutdown */
   11070                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGQUIT, startupproc_quickdie);                /* hard crash time */
   11071                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGALRM, SIG_IGN);
   11072                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
   11073                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGUSR1, SIG_IGN);
   11074         [ +  + ]:         44 :         if (passNum == 1)
   11075                 :         12 :                 pqsignal(SIGUSR2, StartupProcTriggerHandler);
   11076                 :            :         else

   11078                 :            : 
   11079                 :            : #ifdef SIGBUS
   11080                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGBUS, HandleCrash);
   11081                 :            : #endif
   11082                 :            : #ifdef SIGILL
   11083                 :         44 :     pqsignal(SIGILL, HandleCrash);
   11084                 :            : #endif
   11085                 :            : #ifdef SIGSEGV
   11086                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGSEGV, HandleCrash);
   11087                 :            : #endif
   11088                 :            : 
   11089                 :            :         /*
   11090                 :            :          * Reset some signals that are accepted by postmaster but not here
   11091                 :            :          */
   11092                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGCHLD, SIG_DFL);
   11093                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGTTIN, SIG_DFL);
   11094                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGTTOU, SIG_DFL);
   11095                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGCONT, SIG_DFL);
   11096                 :         44 :         pqsignal(SIGWINCH, SIG_DFL);
   11097                 :            :

   11101                 :         44 :         PG_SETMASK(&UnBlockSig);
   11102                 :            : 
   11103   [ +  -  +  +  :         44 :         switch (passNum)
                      - ]

   11105                 :            :         case 1:
   11106                 :         12 :                 StartupXLOG();
   11107                 :         12 :                 break;
   11108                 :            : 
   11109                 :            :         case 2:
   11110                 :            :         case 4:
   11111                 :            :                 /*
   11112                 :            :                  * NOTE: The following initialization logic was borrowed from ftsprobe.
   11113                 :            :                  */
   11114 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         22 :                 SetProcessingMode(InitProcessing);
   11115                 :            : 
   11116                 :            :                 /*
   11117                 :            :                  * Create a resource owner to keep track of our resources (currently only
   11118                 :            :                  * buffer pins).
   11119                 :            :                  */
   11120         [ +  + ]:         22 :                 if (passNum == 2)
   11121                 :            :                 {
   11122                 :         10 :                         CurrentResourceOwner = ResourceOwnerCreate(NULL, "Startup Pass 2");
   11123                 :         10 :                 }
   11124                 :            :                 else
   11125                 :            :                 {
   11126 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         12 :                         Assert(passNum == 4);
   11127                 :         12 :                         CurrentResourceOwner = ResourceOwnerCreate(NULL, "Startup Pass 4");
   11128                 :            :                 }
   11129                 :            : 
   11130                 :            :                 /*
   11131                 :            :                  * NOTE: AuxiliaryProcessMain has already called:
   11132                 :            :                  * NOTE:      BaseInit,
   11133                 :            :                  * NOTE:      InitAuxiliaryProcess instead of InitProcess, and
   11134                 :            :                  * NOTE:      InitBufferPoolBackend.
   11135                 :            :                  */
   11136                 :            : 
   11137                 :         22 :                 InitXLOGAccess();
   11138                 :            : 
   11139 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         22 :                 SetProcessingMode(NormalProcessing);
   11140                 :            : 
   11141                 :            :                 /*
   11142                 :            :                  * Add my PGPROC struct to the ProcArray.
   11143                 :            :                  *
   11144                 :            :                  * Once I have done this, I am visible to other backends!
   11145                 :            :                  */
   11146                 :         22 :                 InitProcessPhase2();
   11147                 :            : 
   11148                 :            :                 /*
   11149                 :            :                  * Initialize my entry in the shared-invalidation manager's array of
   11150                 :            :                  * per-backend data.
   11151                 :            :                  *
   11152                 :            :                  * Sets up MyBackendId, a unique backend identifier.
   11153                 :            :                  */
   11154                 :         22 :                 MyBackendId = InvalidBackendId;
   11155                 :            : 
   11156                 :            :                 /*
   11157                 :            :                  * Though this is a startup process and currently no one sends invalidation
   11158                 :            :                  * messages concurrently, we set sendOnly = false, since we have relcaches.
   11159                 :            :                  */
   11160                 :         22 :                 SharedInvalBackendInit(false);
   11161                 :            : 
   11162 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         22 :                 if (MyBackendId > MaxBackends || MyBackendId <= 0)
   11163                 :          0 :                         elog(FATAL, "bad backend id: %d", MyBackendId);
   11164                 :            : 
   11165                 :            :                 /*
   11166                 :            :                  * bufmgr needs another initialization call too
   11167                 :            :                  */
   11168                 :         22 :                 InitBufferPoolBackend();
   11169                 :            : 
   11170                 :            :                 /* heap access requires the rel-cache.
   11171                 :            :                  *
   11172                 :            :                  * Pass 2 uses heap API to insert/update/delete from persistent
   11173                 :            :                  * tables.  In order to use the heap API, RelationDescriptor is
   11174                 :            :                  * required.  In pass 2, persistent tables are accessed using
   11175                 :            :                  * DirectOpen API to obtain the RelationDescriptor.  Hence, we
   11176                 :            :                  * don't need to load full relcache as in
   11177                 :            :                  * RelationCacheInitializePhase3().
   11178                 :            :                  *
   11179                 :            :                  * However, there is cache invalidation logic within heap API
   11180                 :            :                  * needs basic data structures for catalog cache to be
   11181                 :            :                  * initialized.  Hence, we need to do RelationCacheInitialize(),
   11182                 :            :                  * InitCatalogCache(), and RelationCacheInitializePhase2()
   11183                 :            :                  * before StartupXLOG_Pass2().
   11184                 :            :                  *
   11185                 :            :                  * Pass 4 needs RelationCacheInitializePhase3() to do catalog
   11186                 :            :                  * validation, after xlog replay is complete.
   11187                 :            :                  */

   11189                 :         22 :                 InitCatalogCache();
   11190                 :            : 
   11191                 :            :                 /*
   11192                 :            :                  * It's now possible to do real access to the system catalogs.
   11193                 :            :                  *
   11194                 :            :                  * Load relcache entries for the system catalogs.  This must create at
   11195                 :            :                  * least the minimum set of "nailed-in" cache entries.
   11196                 :            :                  */

   11198                 :            :

   11202                 :         10 :                 }

   11204                 :            :                 {

   11209                 :         22 :                 break;
   11210                 :            : 
   11211                 :            :         case 3:
   11212                 :            :                 /*

   11214                 :            :                  */

   11216                 :            : 
   11217                 :            :                 /*

   11237                 :         10 :                 break;
   11238                 :            : 
   11239                 :            :         default:

   11241                 :          0 :         }

   11370                 :            : static
   11371                 :            : int XLogFillZero(

   11375                 :            :                                  uint32 endOffset)
   11376                 :            : {

   11378                 :            :         char            fname[MAXPGPATH];
   11379                 :            :         char            zbuffer[XLOG_BLCKSZ];
   11380                 :            : 
   11381                 :          0 :         int                     fd = 0;
   11382                 :          0 :         uint32          offset = startOffset;
   11383                 :          0 :         Size            writeLen = 0;
   11384                 :            : 
   11385                 :          0 :         int                     status = STATUS_OK;
   11386                 :          0 :         char            *xlogDir = NULL;
   11387                 :            : 
   11388 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(startOffset < endOffset);
   11389                 :            : 
   11390                 :          0 :         errno = 0;
   11391                 :            : 
   11392                 :          0 :         XLogFileName(fname, ThisTimeLineID, logId, seg);
   11393                 :            : 
   11394                 :          0 :         xlogDir = makeRelativeToTxnFilespace(XLOGDIR);
   11395         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (snprintf(path, MAXPGPATH, "%s/%s", xlogDir, fname) >= MAXPGPATH) {
   11396         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(WARNING,
   11397                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
   11398                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not allocate path, path too long \"%s/%s\"",
   11399                 :            :                                                 xlogDir, fname)));

   11401                 :            :         }
   11402                 :          0 :         pfree(xlogDir);
   11403                 :            : 
   11404                 :          0 :         fd = open(path, O_RDWR, 0);
   11405         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (fd < 0) {
   11406         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(WARNING,
   11407                 :            :                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),

   11409                 :            :                                                         path)));
   11410                 :          0 :                         return STATUS_ERROR;
   11411                 :            :         }
   11412                 :            : 
   11413         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (ftruncate(fd, startOffset) < 0) {
   11414         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(WARNING,
   11415                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
   11416                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not truncate xlog file \"%s\" to position \"%d\" : %m",
   11417                 :            :                                                 path, startOffset)));
   11418                 :          0 :                 status = STATUS_ERROR;
   11419                 :          0 :                 goto exit;
   11420                 :            :         }
   11421                 :            : 
   11422         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (lseek(fd, (off_t) startOffset, SEEK_SET) < 0) {
   11423         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(WARNING,
   11424                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
   11425                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not seek xlog file \"%s\" to position \"%d\" : %m",
   11426                 :            :                                                 path, startOffset)));
   11427                 :          0 :                 status = STATUS_ERROR;
   11428                 :          0 :                 goto exit;
   11429                 :            :         }
   11430                 :            : 
   11431                 :            :         /*
   11432                 :            :          * Zero-fill the file.  We have to do this the hard way to ensure that all
   11433                 :            :          * the file space has really been allocated --- on platforms that allow
   11434                 :            :          * "holes" in files, just seeking to the end doesn't allocate intermediate
   11435                 :            :          * space.  This way, we know that we have all the space and (after the
   11436                 :            :          * fsync below) that all the indirect blocks are down on disk.  Therefore,
   11437                 :            :          * fdatasync(2) or O_DSYNC will be sufficient to sync future writes to the
   11438                 :            :          * log file.
   11439                 :            :          */

                 [ #  # ]
   11441                 :            : 
   11442                 :          0 :         while (1) {
   11443                 :          0 :                 errno = 0;
   11444         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 writeLen = (Size) Min(XLOG_BLCKSZ - (offset % XLOG_BLCKSZ), endOffset - offset);
   11445                 :            : 
   11446         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if ((int) write(fd, zbuffer, writeLen) != (int) writeLen) {
   11447                 :          0 :                         int                     save_errno = errno;
   11448                 :            : 
   11449                 :            :                         /*
   11450                 :            :                          * If we fail to make the file, delete it to release disk space
   11451                 :            :                          */
   11452                 :            : 
   11453                 :            :                         /* if write didn't set errno, assume problem is no disk space */

   11455                 :            : 
   11456         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(WARNING,
   11457                 :            :                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),
   11458                 :            :                                          errmsg("could not write to file \"%s\": %m", path)));
   11459                 :          0 :                         status = STATUS_ERROR;
   11460                 :          0 :                         goto exit;
   11461                 :            :                 }

   11463         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (offset >= endOffset) {
   11464                 :          0 :                         break;
   11465                 :            :                 }
   11466                 :            :         }
   11467                 :            : 
   11468         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (pg_fsync(fd) != 0) {

   11470                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
   11471                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not fsync file \"%s\": %m", path)));
   11472                 :          0 :                 status = STATUS_ERROR;
   11473                 :          0 :         }
   11474                 :            : 
   11475                 :            : exit:

   11477         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(WARNING,
   11478                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
   11479                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not close file \"%s\": %m", path)));
   11480                 :          0 :                 status = STATUS_ERROR;
   11481                 :          0 :         }
   11482                 :            : 
   11483                 :          0 :         return status;
   11484                 :          0 : }
   11485                 :            : 
   11486                 :            : 
   11487                 :            : /*
   11488                 :            :  *
   11489                 :            :  *              a) get logical XLog EOF on primary
   11490                 :            :  *              b) send logical XLog EOF to mirror
   11491                 :            :  *              c) if mirror ahead then reconcile XLog EOF on mirror
   11492                 :            :  *              d) if primary ahead then reconcile XLog EOF on primary

   11511                 :            :         }

   11514                 :            :

   11530                 :          0 :                 return STATUS_OK;

   11540                 :          0 :                 return STATUS_OK;
   11541                 :            :         }

   11547                 :          0 :                                                         simpleFileName);

   11594                 :            : /*
   11595                 :            :  * The routine recovers pg_control flat file on mirror side.

   11597                 :            :  *      b) pg_control file is overwritten on mirror

   11601                 :            : int

   11608                 :            : 
   11609                 :          0 :                 ReadControlFile();
   11610                 :            : 
   11611                 :          0 :                 retval = MirroredFlatFile_Open(
   11612                 :            :                                                           &mirroredOpen,
   11613                 :            :                                                           XLOG_CONTROL_FILE_SUBDIR,
   11614                 :            :                                                           XLOG_CONTROL_FILE_SIMPLE,
   11615                 :            :                                                           O_CREAT | O_RDWR | PG_BINARY,
   11616                 :            :                                                           S_IRUSR | S_IWUSR,
   11617                 :            :                                                           /* suppressError */ false,
   11618                 :            :                                                           /* atomic operation */ false,
   11619                 :            :                                                           /*isMirrorRecovery */ TRUE);
   11620         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (retval != 0)
   11621                 :          0 :                         break;
   11622                 :            : 
   11623                 :          0 :                 retval = MirroredFlatFile_Write(

   11625                 :            :                                                            0,
   11626                 :          0 :                                                            ControlFile,

   11628                 :            :                                                            /* suppressError */ false);

   11632                 :          0 :                 retval = MirroredFlatFile_Flush(

   11634                 :            :                                                            /* suppressError */ false);
   11635         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (retval != 0)
   11636                 :          0 :                         break;
   11637                 :            : 
   11638                 :          0 :                 MirroredFlatFile_Close(&mirroredOpen);
   11639                 :          0 :                 break;
   11640                 :            :         } // while(1)
   11641                 :            : 
   11642                 :          0 :         return retval;

   11645                 :            : /*
   11646                 :            :  * The ChangeTracking module will call this xlog routine in order for
   11647                 :            :  * it to gather all the xlog records since the last checkpoint and

   11654                 :            :  * for more information.

   11665                 :          0 :         int count = 0;
   11666                 :            : 
   11667                 :            :         /*
   11668                 :            :          * Find latest checkpoint record and the redo record from xlog. This record

   11670                 :            :          * to changetracking log. This is needed either to generate/produce new change
   11671                 :            :          * tracking log or to make the changetracking log catchup with xlog in case
   11672                 :            :          * it has fallen behind.

   11678                 :            :                                                  &redoCheckpoint);
   11679                 :            : 
   11680                 :          0 :         startLoc = redoCheckpoint.redo;
   11681                 :            : 
   11682                 :          0 :         XLogCloseReadRecord();
   11683                 :          0 :         elog(LOG, "last checkpoint location for generating initial changetracking log %s",
   11684                 :            :                         XLogLocationToString(&startLoc));
   11685                 :            : 
   11686                 :            :         /*
   11687                 :            :          * Find the last entry and thus the LSN recorded by it from the CT_FULL
   11688                 :            :          * log. Later, it will be used to maintain the xlog and changetracking log
   11689                 :            :          * to the same end point.
   11690                 :            :          * We perform this when the lastChangetrackingEndLoc is not known.
   11691                 :            :          */

   11693                 :            :         {

   11695                 :            :                 {
   11696                 :          0 :                         return 0;
   11697                 :            :                 }
   11698                 :            :                 
   11699                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG, "last changetracked location in changetracking full log %s",
   11700                 :            :                                 XLogLocationToString(&lastChangeTrackingLogEndLoc));
   11701                 :          0 :         }
   11702                 :            : 
   11703                 :          0 :         record = XLogReadRecord(&startLoc, false, LOG);
   11704         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (record == NULL)
   11705                 :            :         {
   11706                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR," couldn't read start location %s",
   11707                 :            :                          XLogLocationToString(&startLoc));
   11708                 :          0 :         }
   11709                 :            : 
   11710 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (lastChangeTrackingEndLoc != NULL &&
   11711 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 XLByteLT(*lastChangeTrackingEndLoc, EndRecPtr))
   11712                 :            :         {
   11713                 :          0 :                 XLogCloseReadRecord();

   11715         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (Debug_persistent_print)

   11722                 :            : 
   11723                 :            :         /*
   11724                 :            :          * Make a pass through all xlog records from last checkpoint and
   11725                 :            :          * gather information from the interesting ones into the change log.

   11727                 :          0 :         while (true)
   11728                 :            :         {
   11729         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (Debug_persistent_print)

   11733                 :            :

   11761                 :            :                         {

   11772                 :            :                         }
   11773                 :          0 :                 }
   11774                 :            :                 else
   11775                 :            :                 {
   11776                 :            :                         /*
   11777                 :            :                          * Read to end of log.
   11778                 :            :                          */
   11779                 :          0 :                         record = XLogReadRecord(NULL, false, LOG);

   11781                 :            :                         {
   11782         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (Debug_persistent_print)
   11783                 :          0 :                                         elog(Persistent_DebugPrintLevel(),
   11784                 :            :                                                  "XLogAddRecordsToChangeTracking: Returning %d records through end of log location %s",
   11785                 :            :                                                  count,
   11786                 :            :                                                  XLogLocationToString(&lastEndLoc));
   11787                 :            : 
   11788                 :          0 :                                 break;
   11789                 :            :                         }
   11790                 :            :                 }
   11791                 :            :         }
   11792                 :            : 
   11793                 :            :         /*
   11794                 :            :          * We now need to make sure that (in the case of crash recovery) there are no
   11795                 :            :          * records in the change tracking logs that have lsn higher than the highest lsn in xlog.
   11796                 :            :          *
   11797                 :            :          *      a) Find the highest lsn in xlog
   11798                 :            :          *      b) Find the highest lsn in change tracking log files before interesting
   11799                 :            :          *         xlog entries from last checkpoint onwards are appended to it
   11800                 :            :          *         (see above)
   11801                 :            :          *      c) if the highest lsn in change tracking > the highest lsn in xlog then
   11802                 :            :          *              i) store in compacting shared memory the highest lsn in xlog
   11803                 :            :          *              ii) Flush all data into CT_LOG_FULL
   11804                 :            :          *              iii) Rename CT_LOG_FULL to CT_LOG_TRANSIENT
   11805                 :            :          *      d) after database is started the compacting (CT_LOG_TRANSIENT) will discard all records from
   11806                 :            :          *         change tracking log file that are higher than the highest lsn in xlog
   11807                 :            :          */

   11809                 :            :         {
   11810                 :            :                 /*
   11811                 :            :                  * Xlog must have been read till the end to get last lsn on
   11812                 :            :                  * disk (EndRecPtr).
   11813                 :            :                  */
   11814 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert (record == NULL);
   11815                 :            : 
   11816 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (! (lastChangeTrackingLogEndLoc.xlogid == 0 && lastChangeTrackingLogEndLoc.xrecoff == 0) &&
                 [ #  # ]
   11817 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         XLByteLT(EndRecPtr, lastChangeTrackingLogEndLoc))
   11818                 :            :                 {
   11819                 :          0 :                         elog(LOG,
   11820                 :            :                                  "changetracking: "
   11821                 :            :                                  "found last changetracking log LSN (%s) higher than last xlog LSN, "
   11822                 :            :                                  "invalid records will be discarded",
   11823                 :            :                                  XLogLocationToString(&lastChangeTrackingLogEndLoc));
   11824                 :            : 
   11825                 :          0 :                         elog(LOG, "xlog LSN (%s)", XLogLocationToString(&EndRecPtr));
   11826                 :            : 
   11827                 :          0 :                         ChangeTracking_FsyncDataIntoLog(CTF_LOG_FULL);
   11828                 :          0 :                         ChangeTrackingSetXLogEndLocation(EndRecPtr);
   11829                 :          0 :                         ChangeTracking_CreateTransientLog();
   11830                 :          0 :                 }
   11831                 :          0 :         }
   11832                 :            : 
   11833                 :          0 :         XLogCloseReadRecord();
   11834                 :          0 :         return count;
   11835                 :          0 : }

   11837                 :            : /*
   11838                 :            :  * The following two gucs
   11839                 :            :  *                                      a) fsync=on
   11840                 :            :  *                                      b) wal_sync_method=open_sync
   11841                 :            :  * open XLOG files with O_DIRECT flag.
   11842                 :            :  * O_DIRECT flag requires XLOG buffer to be 512 byte aligned.
   11843                 :            :  */

   11845                 :            : XLogInitMirroredAlignedBuffer(int32 bufferLen)
   11846                 :            : {
   11847         [ +  + ]:     511320 :         if (bufferLen > writeBufLen)
   11848                 :            :         {
   11849         [ +  + ]:       4348 :                 if (writeBuf != NULL)
   11850                 :            :                 {
   11851                 :       2597 :                         pfree(writeBuf);
   11852                 :       2597 :                         writeBuf = NULL;
   11853                 :       2597 :                         writeBufAligned = NULL;
   11854                 :       2597 :                         writeBufLen = 0;
   11855                 :       2597 :                 }
   11856                 :       4348 :         }
   11857                 :            : 
   11858         [ +  + ]:     511320 :         if (writeBuf == NULL)
   11859                 :            :         {

   11861                 :            :

   11863         [ -  + ]:       4348 :                 if (writeBuf == NULL)
   11864         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
   11865                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_OUT_OF_MEMORY),
   11866                 :            :                                          (errmsg("could not allocate memory for mirrored aligned buffer"))));
   11867                 :       4348 :                 writeBufAligned = (char *) TYPEALIGN(ALIGNOF_XLOG_BUFFER, writeBuf);
   11868                 :       4348 :         }
   11869                 :     511320 : }

   11873                 :            : XLogRecoverMirror(void)

   11897                 :            : 
   11898                 :            : /*
   11899                 :            :  * Check to see whether the user-specified trigger file exists and whether a
   11900                 :            :  * promote request has arrived.  If either condition holds, request postmaster
   11901                 :            :  * to shut down walreceiver, wait for it to exit, and return true.
   11902                 :            :  */
   11903                 :            : static bool
   11904                 :            : CheckForStandbyTrigger(void)
   11905                 :            : {
   11906                 :            :         static bool triggered = false;
   11907                 :            : 
   11908         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (triggered)
   11909                 :          0 :                 return true;
   11910                 :            : 
   11911         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (CheckPromoteSignal(true))
   11912                 :            :         {
   11913         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(LOG,
   11914                 :            :                                 (errmsg("received promote request")));
   11915                 :          0 :                 ShutdownWalRcv();
   11916                 :          0 :                 triggered = true;
   11917                 :          0 :                 return true;
   11918                 :            :         }
   11919                 :            : 
   11920                 :          0 :         return false;
   11921                 :          0 : }
   11922                 :            : 
   11923                 :            : /*
   11924                 :            :  * Check to see if a promote request has arrived. Should be
   11925                 :            :  * called by postmaster after receiving SIGUSR1.
   11926                 :            :  */
   11927                 :            : bool
   11928                 :            : CheckPromoteSignal(bool do_unlink)
   11929                 :            : {
   11930                 :            :         struct stat stat_buf;
   11931                 :            : 
   11932         [ -  + ]:    6400080 :         if (stat(PROMOTE_SIGNAL_FILE, &stat_buf) == 0)
   11933                 :            :         {
   11934                 :            :                 /*
   11935                 :            :                  * Since we are in a signal handler, it's not safe to elog. We
   11936                 :            :                  * silently ignore any error from unlink.
   11937                 :            :                  */
   11938         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (do_unlink)
   11939                 :          0 :                         unlink(PROMOTE_SIGNAL_FILE);
   11940                 :          0 :                 return true;
   11941                 :            :         }
   11942                 :    6400080 :         return false;
   11943                 :    6400080 : }
   11944                 :            : 
   11945                 :            : /*
   11946                 :            :  * Wake up startup process to replay newly arrived WAL, or to notice that
   11947                 :            :  * failover has been requested.
   11948                 :            :  */

   11992                 :            : /*
   11993                 :            :  * True if we are running standby-mode continuous recovery.
